## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬¢, E_╬¢)`** ΓÇö finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal ΓÇö not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, Γäñ^d, ΓÇª) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬º3, ΓÇª) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬¢ : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬¢]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `Γäñ` or `Γäñ^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬¢ ΓåÆ ╬¢ ΓåÆ Γäé` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

Marshall sign conjugation forward (#853) + inverse (#854) gives a real-form sector eigenvector existence with Marshall sign structure. Bundled real-form ground-state theorems: same-`╬╝` form (#855) and forced-eigenvalue form (#859). Complex-form bridge: complex sector matrix Hermiticity + real-Γåö-complex eigenvector correspondence (#857, #858, #861). Complex-form existence (#860), Marshall-positive uniqueness (#862), and **strongest bundled COMPLEX ground-state theorem** `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full` (#865) ΓÇö the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the magnetization sector. Generic spin `S`, arbitrary bipartite-antiferromagnetic Heisenberg coupling supported on a connected bipartite graph, with the intermediate-existence hypothesis. | P1n (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, final statement) | **Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3 (LiebΓÇôMattis, general spin-S, `\|A\| Γëá \|┬¼A\|`) ΓÇö final statement.** The current `Prop` definition `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` is the structural, `h_intermediate`-free statement: it closes the conclusion from the physical hypotheses `(1 Γëñ N, 1 Γëñ \|A\|, 1 Γëñ \|┬¼A\|)` rather than the older vacuous-at-`N=1` intermediate-support hypothesis. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 | Truly-unconditional closure at all `N ΓëÑ 1` (PR #3891 toy, PR #3893 general) |
| P1o (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, MattisΓÇôNishimori anisotropic) | **Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4 (MattisΓÇôNishimori)**: ground-state uniqueness + `┼£┬│_tot \|╬ª_GSΓƒ⌐ = 0` for the anisotropic XXZ + single-ion Hamiltonian (2.5.14) on a connected bipartite lattice with `\|A\| = \|B\|`, under (i) `ΓêÆ1 < ╬╗ Γëñ 1, D ΓëÑ 0` or (ii) `╬╗ ΓëÑ 1, D Γëñ 0`. The model is only U(1)-invariant (not SU(2)), so the proof uses even/odd-sector PerronΓÇôFrobenius (at most double degeneracy) + a deformation argument from the SU(2) point `(╬╗,D)=(1,0)` rather than MLM directly. bridge giving per-block submatrix-full intersection finrank equality, (h.2) #3841 strengthens involution decomposition to finrank equality, (h.3) #3842 combines (h.1)+(h.2) into `finrank Γäé (eig M ╬╝) = Γêæ_p finrank Γäé (eig M.submatrix_p ╬╝)` for parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `P`, (h.4) #3844 submatrix-based Γëñ 2 wrappers. **(i) chain: Hermitian spectral chain** (#3846ΓÇô#3855): #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= ΓèÑ` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `Γëñ 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `Γëñ 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `─ñ'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 `hermitianMinEigenvalue`. **(j) chain: PF eigenvalue identification** (#3857ΓÇô#3861): (j.1) #3857 PF positive eigenvector exists for unshifted dressed submatrix, (j.2) #3858 lift to complex, (j.3) #3859 `hermitianMinEigenvalue Γëñ ╬╝` from eigenvector existence, (j.4) #3860 packaged form, (j.5) #3861 consumer-friendly conditional bound at `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (needs the `╬╜_PF = hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzΓÇôWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank Γäé Γëñ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D ΓëÑ 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D ΓëÑ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬¢|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 Γëñ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ ΓëÑ 1`, `D Γëñ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M Γê⌐ Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M Γê⌐ Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t Γêê Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 Γëñ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D ΓëÑ 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D Γëñ 0`. |

**frustration-free conditions (`TasakiFlatBandFrustrationFree.lean`, toward uniqueness)**: any flat-band ground state `v` (`rayleighOnVec ─ñ v=0`, `t,U>0`) satisfies `b╠é_{u,╧â}v=0` (eq. 11.3.11) and `n╠é_{xΓåæ}n╠é_{xΓåô}v=0` (no-double-occupancy form of 11.3.12), since `─ñ` is a sum of PSD terms each of which must annihilate a zero-energy state; and any ground state lies in the Hubbard hard-core subspace (`flatBand_groundState_mem_hardcoreSubspace`); **uniqueness framework subspaces (`TasakiFlatBandSubspaces.lean`)**: `flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule` (span of ╬▒-Slater states, contains `|╬ª╬▒Γƒ⌐`) and `flatBandBKernelSubmodule = Γ¿à_{u,╧â} ker b╠é_{u,╧â}` (contains every ground state, from 11.3.11) ΓÇö Tasaki's uniqueness is the inclusion `BKernel Γèå ╬▒Fock` + symmetric/maximal-spin classification; **rotated-basis Fock-spanning infrastructure toward it (`TasakiFlatBandModeCreation.lean`, `TasakiFlatBandModeMonomial.lean`, `Math/ListProdMulVec.lean`, Issue #4346)**: the single-particle-mode creation/annihilation maps `─êΓÇá_╧â(w)`/`─ê_╧â(w)` (`flatBandModeCreation`/`flatBandModeAnnihilation`, with ├óΓÇá/b╠éΓÇá/├ó/b╠é as values at ╬▒/╬▓), the operator-level single-particle change of basis (`flatBandModeCreation_eq_repr_sum`), the generic single-particle CAR `{─ê_╧â(w),─êΓÇá_╧ä(w')}=(Γêæ_x w(x)w'(x))╬┤_╧â╧ä┬╖1` (`flatBandMode_annihilation_creation_anticomm`, giving the ╬▓-Gram and `{b╠é,├óΓÇá}=0`), and `flatBandModeFockSubmodule_eq_top` ΓÇö the rotated-basis Fock monomials `(ΓêÅ ─êΓÇá_╧â(basis i))|vacΓƒ⌐` span the whole space (every `basisVec c` is an ordered site-creation product on the vacuum, and the span is invariant under each site creation), reindexed by occupation configs (card `2^(4K+4)=finrank`) into the **rotated occupation basis** `flatBandOccBasis`; **the hard inclusion `BKernel Γèå AlphaFock` is now PROVED** (`flatBandBKernelSubmodule_le_alphaFockSubmodule`, `TasakiFlatBandUniqueness.lean`, axiom-clean): the ╬▓-Gram is invertible (PosDef, `flatBandBetaGram`), its inverse gives dual annihilators `d_{u,╧â}=Γêæ_v(GΓü╗┬╣)_{uv}b╠é_{v,╧â}` with `{d,b╠éΓÇá}=╬┤`, `{d,├óΓÇá}=0`, `d|vacΓƒ⌐=0`; the projector `b╠éΓÇá_{u,╧â}d_{u,╧â}` kills a b╠é-kernel vector (since `d v=0`), forcing every ╬▓-occupied occupation-basis coordinate to vanish, so the vector is a combination of ╬▓-free occ monomials (= ╬▒-Slater states) Γêê ╬▒-Fock; with the easy inclusion, `BKernel = AlphaFock`; **dimension-route reduction of the uniqueness `Γëñ` half (`TasakiFlatBandClassification.lean`, Issue #4346)**: `finrank(multiplet)=K+2`, `[┼£^z_tot,─ñ_flat]=0` (`fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_flatBandHamiltonian`, from `[┼£^┬▒,─ñ]=0` + su(2) `┼£Γü║┼£Γü╗ΓêÆ┼£Γü╗┼£Γü║=2┼£^z`), `┼£^z` preserves the ground subspace, the finite `┼£^z`-weight decomposition `G=Γ¿å_{a:Fin(K+2)} GΓèôeigenspace(┼£^z, aΓêÆ(K+1)/2)` (`flatBandHalfFilledGroundSubmodule_eq_iSup_weight`, off-weight blocks `ΓèÑ` since `N╠é=K+1` fixes the half-integer weights), and the capstone `flatBand_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan_of_blocks` ΓÇö IF each `┼£^z`-weight block of `G` is `Γëñ1`-dimensional THEN `G=multiplet` (via `Math/EigenspaceWeightFinrank.finrank_le_of_weight_blocks` + `Submodule.eq_of_le_of_finrank_le`, using the proven `multiplet Γëñ G`); the residual `finrank(block)Γëñ1` (symmetric/maximal-spin core) and removal of the axiom are subsequent steps; **easy inclusion (`TasakiFlatBandAlphaFockKernel.lean`)**: `╬▒Fock Γëñ BKernel` (`flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule_le_BKernelSubmodule`) ΓÇö every ╬▒-Slater state is annihilated by all `b╠é` via the 11.3.7 anticommutation + move-through; `ΓëÑ` (existence) fully proven, `Γëñ` (uniqueness) uses a **single documented `axiom`** (Tasaki App. A classification, deferred like Theorem 11.8; existence half is axiom-free); **┬º11.3.2 Mielke's flat-band ferromagnetism (`MielkeHamiltonian.lean`, Issue #4177)**: `mielkeHamiltonian` on a graph (uniform hopping + `2t┬╖N╠é` shift + `U`) with Hermiticity, `[─ñ,N╠é]=0`, SU(2) invariance ΓÇö model setup; The Theorem 11.11 occupation-basis machinery, re-developed generically in the basis for instantiation at the spectral eigenbasis of `T`. **Theorem 11.17 PR3 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandEigenbasis.lean`): spectral-eigenbasis annihilation peel** ΓÇö the orthonormal eigenbasis `T.IsHermitian.eigenvectorBasis` transported to a `Module.Basis` of `Fin(M+1)ΓåÆΓäé` (`eigenbasisAsBasis`), its coordinate orthonormality `╬ú_x conj(e_j(x))e_k(x)=╬┤_{jk}`, the dual CAR `{─ê_╧â(─ô_j),─êΓÇá_╧ä(e_k)}=╬┤_{jk}╬┤_{╧â╧ä}┬╖1`, the smeared-annihilator vacuum kill `─ê_╧â(╧å)|vacΓƒ⌐=0`, and the eigenbasis-annihilation peel `─ê_╧â(─ô_j)|qsΓƒ⌐ = ╬úß╡ó (-1)^i┬╖╬┤_{j,(qs[i]).1}╬┤_{╧â,(qs[i]).2}┬╖|qsΓêûiΓƒ⌐` (the occupation-detection engine). **Theorem 11.17 PR4 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpanning.lean`): eq. (11.3.46) Fock spanning (hard direction)** ΓÇö the eigenvalue equation for the transported eigenbasis, the ground-state annihilation `─ê_╧â(─ô_j)╬ª=0` for every nonzero-eigenvalue mode, the eigenmode number operator `n╠é_{j,╧â}=─êΓÇá_╧â(e_j)─ê_╧â(─ô_j)` with its creation commutation `[n╠é,─êΓÇá_╧ä(e_k)]=╬┤_{jk}╬┤_{╧â╧ä}─êΓÇá_╧ä(e_k)`, its diagonality in the occupation basis `n╠é_{j,╧â}occMon(g)=g(j,╧â)┬╖occMon(g)` (list induction), the **coefficient vanishing** (ground-state occupation-basis coefficient = 0 on any config occupying a nonzero-eigenvalue mode), and the capstone **`flatBand_groundState_mem_flatFockSpan`**: a flat-band ground state lies in the span of the flat-band-supported (zero-eigenvalue) occupation monomials (`IsFlatSupported`). **Theorem 11.17 PR5 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandFilling.lean`): filling-refined eq. (11.3.46)** ΓÇö the total fermion number operator is diagonal in the occupation basis (`N╠é occMon(g)=|occupied(g)|┬╖occMon(g)`, list induction via `[N╠é,─êΓÇá(w)]=─êΓÇá(w)`), so a `DΓéÇ`-electron ground state has occupation-basis coefficients supported on the `DΓéÇ`-electron configs; combined with the flat-band coefficient vanishing this gives `flatBand_groundState_atFilling_mem_flatFockSpan` (flat-band ground state Γêê span of flat-supported occupation monomials with exactly `DΓéÇ` occupied modes). **Theorem 11.17 PR6 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandMuTransport.lean`): transport of eq. (11.3.49) characterization, placing per-block weight states into ground (PR #4448). `upCountOn_comp_swap_eq` (in-block transposition preserves a block's up-count) + `generalFlatBand_blockWeightState_mem_groundSubmodule`: the per-block fiber sum W_{p,q} = ╬ú_{upCount A=p, upCount Aß╢£=q} Slater(extend s) is a ground state, since its indicator coefficient is edge-swap-invariant (basis-graph edges lie within one block, so swaps preserve both block up-counts) (PR #4449). `exists_blockUpCount_config` (a config with prescribed per-block up-counts) + `generalFlatBand_disconnected_finrank_gt`: **┬¼connected Γƒ╣ finrank ground > DΓéÇ+1** ΓÇö the (|A|+1)(|Aß╢£|+1) weight states W_{p,q} are linearly independent (at a fiber representative's index config, only its W has a nonzero occupation coordinate, via `idxConfigOf_flatBandSpinConfigList_inj_gen`), so finrank ΓëÑ (|A|+1)(|Aß╢£|+1) > DΓéÇ+1; the contradiction with the multiplet's finrank=DΓéÇ+1 (PR #4450). **`generalFlatBand_theorem_11_17` (CAPSTONE, AXIOM DISCHARGED)**: `generalFlatBandFerromagnetic T U Γåö generalFlatBandBasisConnected I ╬╝` ΓÇö ΓçÉ via `generalFlatBand_connected_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`, ΓçÆ via the `┬¼connected Γƒ╣ finrank > DΓéÇ+1` contrapositive; the ┬º11.3.4 Theorem 11.17 axiom is now a proved theorem depending only on `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound` (PR #4451). adjacent-swap bridge is in place** (`TJAdjacentSwap.lean`): an `AdjacentSwapStep` (exchange the values at an adjacent pair of distinct sites, `= s Γêÿ Equiv.swap a b`) is always a `TJStep` (`tjReachable_of_adjacentSwapReachable`); the sector connectivity then reduces to the model-agnostic combinatorial fact that two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable. **The reachability basics are in place** (`TJSwapReachableBasics.lean`): `adjacentSwapReachable_swap` (the value-swapped `s Γêÿ Equiv.swap a b` is reachable), `adjacentSwapReachable_count` bubble target). **The sector connectivity is now proven** (`TJSwapReachable.lean`): `bubble_reachable` (move a value leftward to a target by NN swaps, prefix-preserving, induction on the gap) + `reach_of_agree_aux` (selection sort: bubble the needed value into the leftmost disagreement, recurse) assemble into **`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`** ΓÇö any two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable, hence (via the bridge) `TJStep`-reachable within a sector. **The shifted PF matrix is set up** (`TJSectorShifted.lean`): `tJSectorShifted = c┬╖1 ΓêÆ M` with (`#Γêà+#Γåæ+#Γåô=N+1`) + `tJSector_same_counts` (distinct sector states share all value-counts) feed the connectivity, and `tJSectorShifted_pow_pos_of_reachable` lifts a `TJStep`-reachable chain to a Ne)` witness (the `┼£┬│=┬╜` filling with `Γåæ`'s left of `Γåô`'s) supplies A.18's `[Nonempty]`, and **`tJEffReMatrixOnSector_perronFrobenius`** applies Theorem A.18 to `M = tJEffReMatrixOnSector` ΓÇö `M` has a strictly positive eigenvector at its lowest eigenvalue `╬╝` (the sector ground energy), with `finrank Γëñ 1` (non-degeneracy): the unique positive PF ground state of the spin-charge-separated sector. **The sectorΓåöfull bridge is started** (`TJExpansion.lean`): `tJExpansion v = ╬ú_s v_sΓÇó\|╬ª_sΓƒ⌐`, `tJExpansionCoeff`, and the left inverse paired with the SU(2)-tower lower bound (`tJ_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge`, #4305) this pins `finrank G = Ne+1`. **Proposition 11.24 is now PROVED** (`proposition_11_24` in `TJProposition1124.lean`, E6 capstone): `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ΓÇª) Ne` ΓÇö `finrank G = Ne+1` (le_antisymm of #4305/#4312) and every ground state is an `(┼£_tot)┬▓` eigenvector at `(Ne/2)(Ne/2+1)` (the `Ne+1` LI tower states `(┼£Γü╗)^k ╬⌐` span `G`, each maximal-spin via `highestWeight_spinMultiplet_general`). **This discharges the former `axiom proposition_11_24`**; the entire d=1 ferromagnetic t-J ferromagnetism result rests only on Theorem A.17 (**now discharged** ┬ºA.3.2). Then the total-spin identification (`S_tot = Ne/2`) via the highest weight raised this way, the SU(2)-tower lower bound (paired with this `┼£┬│=┬╜` block bound for the upper bound), and the capstone replacing the axiom. The discharge lemmas add no new t-J axioms; the only inherited dependency is the A.17 sector reduction `tJHamiltonian_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (which rests on Theorem A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`, **now discharged**) axiom), every other layer being fully axiom-free.**); **generic fixed-filling hard-core ground subspace (`GroundSubspaceAtFilling.lean`)**: `fillingHardcoreStates`/`groundEnergyAtFilling`/`groundSubmoduleAtFilling` (`H`-eigenspace at the ground energy Γèô `N╠é=Ne` Γèô `hubbardHardcoreSubspace`), shared by Prop 11.24 and Thm 11.26; axiom-free; **┬º11.5.3 d=1 decorated Hubbard model + Lemma 11.25 (documented axiom) + **Theorem 11.26 (PROVED, `MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314)**: the duplicated-internal-site lattice `╬¢=EΓê¬(I├ù{1,2})` in `Fin(3K+3)` (`decExternalSite`/`decInternalSite1/2`); localized states `decAlpha`/`decBeta1`/`decBeta2` (eqs. (11.5.7)ΓÇô(11.5.9)) + fermion ops `decA/BCreation/Annihilation` (eq. (11.5.10)); `decHopping` (`t ╬ú b╠éΓÇáb╠é ΓêÆ s ╬ú_{Γƒ¿p,qΓƒ⌐ΓêêE,╧â}├óΓÇá_p├ó_q`, eq. (11.5.14)) + `decInteraction` (`U ╬ú n╠éΓåæn╠éΓåô`, eq. (11.5.13)) + `decHubbardHamiltonian`; **`axiom lemma_11_25`** (`t,UΓåæΓê₧` Hubbard Γëí `JΓåæΓê₧` t-J at `╧ä=(1+4╬╜┬▓)s`, rendered as spin-structure transfer: Hubbard ground subspace is the maximal-spin `Ne+1`-multiplet **iff** the t-J one is, in the limits; **discharge in progress, Issue #4314**: with Prop 11.24 proved, the first step `TJHalfFillingKinetic.lean` proves the t-J kinetic term vanishes at half-filling `Ne=K+1` ΓÇö `tJ_kinetic_sandwich_mulVec_tJConfigOf_eq_zero_of_full`, `P╠éhc K P╠éhc |╬ª_sΓƒ⌐=0` for fully-occupied `s`, so `─ñ_tJ|half` reduces to the ferromagnetic Heisenberg model; PR2 `TJHalfFillingExchange.lean` completes the reduction `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_tJConfigOf_eq_of_full`: `─ñ_tJ|╬ª_sΓƒ⌐ = J┬╖tJExchange|╬ª_sΓƒ⌐` for fully-occupied `s`; PR3a `TJAllUpSpinDot.lean` computes `fermionSpinDot_mulVec_allUpState` `┼£_i┬╖┼£_j|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐=┬╝|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐` (iΓëáj), so the all-up state has exchange energy 0; PR3b `TJAllUpGround.lean` then proves `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState_eq_zero` `─ñ_tJ|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐=0` at half-filling ΓÇö the maximal-spin `|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐` is a zero-energy eigenstate; PR3c-prep `TJSingletAnnihilation.lean` adds the singlet annihilator `╬ö_xy=─ë_{yΓåô}─ë_{xΓåæ}ΓêÆ─ë_{yΓåæ}─ë_{xΓåô}` (`╬ö_xy|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐=0`), towards the bond identity; PR3c `TJExchangeBondPSD.lean` proves the CAR identity `tJSingletAnnihilation_conjTranspose_mul_self` `╬ö_xyΓÇá╬ö_xy = n╠é_{xΓåæ}n╠é_{yΓåô}+n╠é_{xΓåô}n╠é_{yΓåæ}ΓêÆ┼£Γü║_x┼£Γü╗_yΓêÆ┼£Γü╗_x┼£Γü║_y` (xΓëáy) + cross-site commutes `TJCrossSiteSpinCommute.lean`, the content behind the half-bond identity; PR3d `TJExchangeBondHalf.lean` proves `tJExchangeBond_eq_half_singletNormSq` `n╠é_xn╠é_y/4ΓêÆ┼£_x┬╖┼£_y=┬╜╬ö_xyΓÇá╬ö_xy` (xΓëáy) and `tJExchangeBond_posSemidef` (the bond is positive-semidefinite) ΓÇö the per-bond PSD input; PR3e `TJExchangePSD.lean` proves `tJExchange_posSemidef` `(tJExchange N G).PosSemidef` (graph sum of per-bond PSD terms via `Finset.sum_induction`) ΓÇö the operator nonnegativity behind the half-filling ground energy 0; PR3f `TJHalfFillingReduction.lean` extends to the whole sector ΓÇö `tJFillingSector_full` (Ne=N+1 Γƒ╣ fully occupied), `tJ_kinetic_sandwich_mulVec_eq_zero_of_filling`, and `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_eq_smul_tJExchange_of_filling` (`─ñ_tJ v=J┬╖tJExchange v` for hard-core N╠é=N+1 v); PR3g `TJAllUpProperties.lean` (`N╠é|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐=(N+1)|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐`, hardcore, Γëá0) + `TJHalfFillingGroundEnergy.lean` `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_zero` (`groundEnergyAtFilling ─ñ_tJ (N+1)=0`: all-up at 0, PSD ΓçÆ rayleighΓëÑ0); axiom-free); PR3h `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyLower.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge` (`N+2 Γëñ finrank` of the half-filling ground subspace: the all-up state is a highest-weight ground state, so the SU(2) lowering tower yields `N+2` LI ground states `(┼£Γü╗)^k|ΓåæΓÇªΓåæΓƒ⌐`; mirrors #4305; axiom-free); PR3i `TJHalfFillingBondAction.lean` `tJExchangeBond_mulVec_tJConfigOf_full` (the exchange bond is a half spin-swap on the filled sector: `(┬╝ n╠é_x n╠é_y ΓêÆ ┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)|╬ª_sΓƒ⌐=┬╜(|╬ª_sΓƒ⌐ΓêÆ|╬ª_{tJSpinSwap s x y}Γƒ⌐)` via the sign-free swap over the 4 spin cases; ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes ΓÇö toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `Γƒ¿v,tJExchange vΓƒ⌐=0` ΓçÆ each `Γƒ¿v,bond_xy vΓƒ⌐=0` ΓçÆ `╬ö_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts Γƒ╣ equal value-counts Γƒ╣ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] Γƒ╣ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G Γëñ N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes Γƒ╣ injective `G Γå¬ (Fin(N+2)ΓåÆΓäé)`; with the lower bound #4326 Γƒ╣ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne Γëñ K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ┬ºA.3.2). (11.5.19)ΓÇô(11.5.22), `├ó` with `1/ΓêÜ(3+4╬╜┬▓)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`╬ú_{Γƒ¿p,qΓƒ⌐}(ΓêÆs ├óΓÇá├óΓêÆt b╠éΓÇáb╠é)+uΓéü╬úb╠éΓÇáb╠é+uΓéé╬úd╠éΓÇád╠é`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uΓéé,UΓåæΓê₧` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`d╠é╬ª=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uΓéé,U=Γê₧` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uΓéü>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1ΓëñNeΓëñ2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dΓëñNΓëñ2L^d`) ΓçÆ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian ΓÇª) Ne Γèô ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` ΓÇö taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uΓéé,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

| Appendix A (Tasaki Mathematical Appendices) | The book-order content after Chapter 11; foundations for the deferred Chapter-11 proof discharges (frustration-free A.9/A.10, limit A.11/A.12, PerronΓÇôFrobenius A.17/A.18 ΓÇö the last largely already in `Math/PerronFrobenius*`/`CollatzWielandt*`). **Complete ΓÇö A.1ΓÇôA.28 all formalized in book order** (Issues [#4205](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4205) + [#4224](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4224), both closed; see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` **now proved (axiom-free)** (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula` ΓÇö mathlib has only the commuting case): generic `trotterProductFormula` in a complete normed `Γä¥`-algebra via exponential-series tail bounds (`ΓÇûe^XΓêÆ1ΓêÆXΓÇû Γëñ ΓÇûXΓÇû┬▓e^{ΓÇûXΓÇû}` etc.) + telescoping power estimate (`ΓÇûCΓü┐ΓêÆDΓü┐ΓÇû Γëñ n┬╖M^{nΓêÆ1}┬╖ΓÇûCΓêÆDΓÇû`) + `O(s┬▓)` product comparison + the exact `n`-th-power identity, instantiated for matrices under the scoped operator norm; **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `├é` is `ΓëÑ0` iff all eigenvalues `ΓëÑ0`), **A.5** (`├é,B╠éΓëÑ0 ΓçÆ ├é+B╠éΓëÑ0`), **A.6** (`B╠éΓÇáB╠éΓëÑ0`, and conversely every `├éΓëÑ0` is `─ê┬▓` for a *unique* PSD square root `─ê=ΓêÜ├é` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `├éΓëñB╠é` ΓçÆ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `├é.eigenvaluesΓéÇ i Γëñ B╠é.eigenvaluesΓéÇ i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the CourantΓÇôFischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `reΓƒ¿x,TxΓƒ⌐=Γêæ╬╗ß╡óΓÇûΓƒ¿bß╡ó,xΓƒ⌐ΓÇû┬▓` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(cardΓêÆi)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`├éΓëñB╠é ΓçÆ Tr e^├é Γëñ Tr e^B╠é`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^├é = ╬ú e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `─ñ=╬ú─ÑΓ▒╝`, `─ÑΓ▒╝ΓëÑ╬╡Γ▒╝`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate ΓçÆ ground state at `╬ú╬╡Γ▒╝`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`├éΓëÑ0 Γêº Γƒ¿╬ª|├é|╬ªΓƒ⌐=0 ΓçÆ ├é╬ª=0`; `├é=B╠éΓÇáB╠é ΓçÆ B╠é╬ª=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `─┤┬▓╬ª=0 ΓçÆ ─┤Γü╜ß╡àΓü╛╬ª=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`─ñ_v=─ñΓéÇ+vV╠é`, `V╠éΓëÑ0`: `vΓåæΓê₧` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `╬ª` satisfy the effective Schr├╢dinger eq `P╠éΓéÇ─ñΓéÇ╬ª=E╬ª` on `HΓéÇ=ker V╠é`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `v┬╖Γƒ¿╬ª_v|V╠é|╬ª_vΓƒ⌐ = E_vΓÇû╬ª_vΓÇû┬▓ΓêÆΓƒ¿╬ª_v|─ñΓéÇ|╬ª_vΓƒ⌐`, the converging right side times `vΓü╗┬╣ΓåÆ0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `V╠é╬ª`; pairing with `╧ê Γêê ker V╠é` removes the `vV╠é` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬ºA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) ΓÇö operator identity `─┤Γü╗─┤Γü║=─┤┬▓ΓêÆ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`ΓêÆJΓëñM<J`, `╬ªΓëá0` ΓçÆ `─┤Γü║╬ªΓëá0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤Γü║╬ªΓêêH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤Γü║]=─┤Γü║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤Γü║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤Γü║─┤Γü╗=─┤┬▓ΓêÆ─┤┬│(─┤┬│ΓêÆ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤Γü╗╬ªΓëá0` for `ΓêÆJ<MΓëñJ`, `─┤Γü╗╬ªΓêêH_{J,MΓêÆ1}`) **proved** ΓÇö A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `ΓÇû─┤┬▒╬ªΓÇû┬▓={J(J+1)ΓêÆM(M┬▒1)}ΓÇû╬ªΓÇû┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`ΓêÆJΓëñMΓëñJ`, i.e. `JΓêÆM,J+MΓëÑ0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤Γü║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JΓêÆM Γêê ΓäñΓëÑ0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,ΓêÆ─┤┬▓,ΓêÆ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M Γêê ΓäñΓëÑ0`, so `2J=(JΓêÆM)+(J+M)ΓêêΓäñΓëÑ0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─ñ` (`[─ñ,─┤ß╡à]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JΓêÆk}` (`kΓëñ2J`) ΓÇö `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is ΓëÑ`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─ñ` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` ΓÇö `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `─┤┬▓` being PSD and commuting with `─┤┬│`/`─ñ`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronΓÇôFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬╖1ΓêÆM)` irreducible (off-diag `Γëñ0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector ΓÇö `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzΓÇôWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VΓÇá`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d ΓëÑ 0`) ΓÇö **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aß┤┤A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `╬╗_i ΓëÑ 0`, `d_i = ΓêÜ╬╗_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iΓü╗┬╣┬╖A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vß┤┤`, `C = V D Vß┤┤` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear Γêù-automorphism of `M(H)` Γƒ╣ `╬ô(├é)=├¢ΓÇá├é├¢`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear Γƒ╣ `├¢ΓÇá├é╠ä├¢`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PΓÇ▓]` Γƒ╣ (anti)unitary `V╠é`) ΓÇö documented axioms over `Matrix D D Γäé` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachΓÇôAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-Γêù-continuous `╧å : WeakDual Γäé A` with `╧å 1 = 1`, `0 Γëñ ╧å(aΓÇáa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-Γêù compact, documented axiom) ΓÇö `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─ñ,┬╖]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 Γëñ ╧ë(aΓÇá┬╖╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `╧ë(aΓÇá╬┤a) ΓëÑ ╬│╧ë(aΓÇáa)` on `╧ë(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state Γƒ║ `╧ë(─ñ_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬¢_L, documented axiom) ΓÇö `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` ΓÇö every state `╧ü` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_╧ü, ╧Ç_╧ü, ╬⌐_╧ü)` (`Γêù`-representation `╧Ç : A ΓåÆΓïåΓéÉ[Γäé] (HΓåÆL H)` + cyclic `╬⌐`) with `╧ü(├é)=Γƒ¿╬⌐,╧Ç(├é)╬⌐Γƒ⌐` and `{╧Ç(├é)╬⌐}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **┬ºA.6ΓÇô┬ºA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete ΓåÆ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1ΓÇôA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬º11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** ΓÇö the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `Γä¥`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vΓü╗┬╣`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4ΓÇôA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7ΓÇôA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantΓÇôFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9ΓÇôA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13ΓÇôA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronΓÇôFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzΓÇôWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19ΓÇôA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** ΓÇö the
  operator-algebraic **A.21ΓÇôA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachΓÇôAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* ΓÇö they record exactly what Tasaki proves ΓÇö but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-Γêù
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬º2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(╧â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(╧â^╬▒)ΓÇá = ╧â^╬▒` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(╧â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `╧â^x ╧â^y = i┬╖╧â^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `╧â^╬▒ ╧â^╬▓ + ╧â^╬▓ ╧â^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ Γëá ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S ΓëÑ 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : Γäò`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬º2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) Γäé`, with `N = 2S : Γäò` (so `N = 1` Γçö `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` Γçö `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬º2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼£^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, ΓÇª, -N/2)` for `N : Γäò` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `ΓêÜ(k┬╖(N ΓêÆ k + 1))` (resp. `ΓêÜ((N ΓêÆ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼£^(1) := (┼£^+ + ┼£^-) / 2`, `┼£^(2) := (┼£^+ ΓêÆ ┼£^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼£^+` annihilates `\|NΓƒ⌐` (highest weight); `┼£^-` annihilates `\|0Γƒ⌐` (lowest weight) ΓÇö the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^+] = ┼£^+` and `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^-] = -┼£^-`: `┼£^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼£^+)ß┤┤ = ┼£^-`, `(┼£^-)ß┤┤ = ┼£^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼£^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ Γêê {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼£^{(1)}` and `┼£^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼£^{(3)} ΓêÆ ╬╗_j ΓÇó 1) ┬╖ P_k = (╬╗_k ΓêÆ ╬╗_j) ┬╖ P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `ΓêÅ_{j Γëá k} (┼£^{(3)} ΓêÆ ╬╗_j ΓÇó 1) / (╬╗_k ΓêÆ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼£^{(3)} ┬╖ P_k = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : Γäé)/2 ΓêÆ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬╖ P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬╖ A`). Specialised to `┼£^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) ΓÇö the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ P_{k+1} ┬╖ ┼£^- = (k + 1)(N ΓêÆ k) ┬╖ P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼£^- ┬╖ P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼£^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬ú_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(Γäé)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = ΓêÜ((i+1)(NΓêÆi)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}` and `┼£^- ┬╖ P_i = ΓêÜ((NΓêÆi)(i+1)) ┬╖ E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼£^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 ΓêÆ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^+ ┬╖ ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ΓêÆ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼£^+[i, l] ┬╖ ┼£^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+` and `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+ = diag(i ┬╖ (N ΓêÆ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼£^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬╖ 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼£^+ ┬╖ ┼£^- + ┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼£^{(3)} ┬╖ \|kΓƒ⌐ = (N/2 ΓêÆ k) ┬╖ \|kΓƒ⌐`, where `\|kΓƒ⌐ := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) ΓåÆ Γäé)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ \|kΓƒ⌐ = ΓêÜ(k(N ΓêÆ k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k ΓêÆ 1Γƒ⌐` for `k ΓëÑ 1`, and `┼£^- ┬╖ \|kΓƒ⌐ = ΓêÜ((N ΓêÆ k)(k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k + 1Γƒ⌐` for `k Γëñ N ΓêÆ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼£)┬▓ ┬╖ \|kΓƒ⌐ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ \|kΓƒ⌐`. Direct consequence of `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` (╬▓-14) ΓÇö every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼£^{(╬▒)}` and `┼£^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼£^+, ┼£^-] = 2 ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼£^+ ┬╖ ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ΓêÆ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+ = diag(i (N ΓêÆ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) ΓêÆ i(N-i+1) = N ΓêÆ 2i = 2(N/2 ΓêÆ i)` matches `2 ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}] = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2` and `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ΓêÆ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) ΓêÆ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼£^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}] = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^┬▒] = ┬▒┼£^┬▒`) via `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ΓêÆ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼£^+ ΓêÆ ┼£^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬╖ 2 ┼£^{(1)} = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^{(1)}] = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼£^+ ΓêÆ ┼£^-) = (1/2) ┬╖ (2i) ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)} = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kΓƒ⌐Γƒ¿k|`: `P_k ┬╖ P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬╖ P_j = 0` for `i Γëá j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`Γêæ_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) ΓÇö lets us pull a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼£^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = ΓêÅ_{j Γëá k} (┼£^{(3)} ΓêÆ ╬╗_j ΓÇó 1)/(╬╗_k ΓêÆ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : Γäé)/2 ΓêÆ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`Γêæ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠é` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼£^+ = ┼£^{(1)} + i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}` and `┼£^- = ┼£^{(1)} ΓêÆ i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼£^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼£^+ + ┼£^-)`, `┼£^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼£^+ ΓêÆ ┼£^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k Γêê Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k Γêê Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼£^+, ┼£^- Γêê Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼£^┬▒ = ┼£^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `Γäé`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} Γêê Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼£^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = ΓêÜ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k Γêê adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼£^┬▒ Γêê adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} Γêê Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬╖ E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **≡ƒÄ» Tasaki ┬º2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `Γäé^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin Γäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}} = Γèñ`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} Γêê adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = Γêæ_{i,j} M_{i,j} ΓÇó E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `╧â : ╬¢ ΓåÆ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼£_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼£_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSiteCore.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body

| Lean name | Statement | File |

| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `╬ÿ╠é|╧ê^ΓåæΓƒ⌐ = |╧ê^ΓåôΓƒ⌐` and `╬ÿ╠é|╧ê^ΓåôΓƒ⌐ = -|╧ê^ΓåæΓƒ⌐` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |

| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `ΓêÅ_x ╬╡(╧ä x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|╬¢|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki ┬º2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `╬ÿ╠é_tot┬▓ = (-1)^|╬¢| ┬╖ 1╠é` ΓÇö `+1╠é` when `|╬¢|` is even, `-1╠é` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x ╧â^x).mulVec |╬¿_╧âΓƒ⌐ = |╬¿_{siteFlipAt ╧â x}Γƒ⌐` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y`** (Tasaki ┬º2.3): `╬ÿ╠é_tot ((┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y) v) = (┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y)(╬ÿ╠é_tot v)` ΓÇö two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `╬ÿ╠é_tot` for any real coupling `J : Γä¥`.
