| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|ΓåæΓåôΓƒ⌐ + |ΓåôΓåæΓƒ⌐` is **anti-invariant** under `╬ÿ╠é_tot`: `╬ÿ╠é_tot (|ΓåæΓåôΓƒ⌐ + |ΓåôΓåæΓƒ⌐) = -(|ΓåæΓåôΓƒ⌐ + |ΓåôΓåæΓƒ⌐)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬¢` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬¢ = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬¢` | `Matrix (╬¢ ΓåÆ Fin 2) (╬¢ ΓåÆ Fin 2) Γäé` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬¢` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian ΓåÆ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x Γëá y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬╖ onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬╖ B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `╧ê` also fixes `╧ê` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A┬╖B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanΓÇôWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### HorschΓÇôvon der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki ┬º3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3ΓÇô10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki ┬º3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)ΓÇô(3.4.12), pp. 66ΓÇô67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `EΓéÇ = eigenvalues iΓéÇ`, a unit state `╬ô` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `Γƒ¿╬ô,H╬ôΓƒ⌐ Γëñ EΓéÇ+╬┤` yields an energy eigenstate `j Γëá iΓéÇ` with `EΓéÇ Γëñ E_j Γëñ EΓéÇ+╬┤` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (┬º3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `╬¿` of `H ΓêÆ h┬╖O` (`h>0`) and any trial `╬₧`, the order parameter obeys `Γƒ¿╬₧,O╬₧Γƒ⌐ + (EΓéÇΓêÆΓƒ¿╬₧,H╬₧Γƒ⌐)/h Γëñ Γƒ¿╬¿,O╬¿Γƒ⌐` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `LΓåæΓê₧,hΓåô0` gives the ΓêÜqΓéÇ SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (┬º4.1, DysonΓÇôLiebΓÇôSimon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (N├⌐el) order operator `├ö = ╬ú_x ╬╡_x ┼£_x^(3)` (`╬╡_x = ┬▒1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real ┬▒1 combination of Hermitian `┼£_x^(3)`), so `Γƒ¿╬ª,├ö┬▓╬ªΓƒ⌐` is a real order parameter. The N├⌐el-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (┬º4.1, Shastry, AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)ΓÇô(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `─ñ_h = ╬ú_x ┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1} ΓêÆ h┬╖├ö_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{hΓåô0} lim_{LΓåæΓê₧}` (eq. (4.1.10), ╬╡ΓÇô╬┤ form). Tasaki states it without proof; the deep argument is now being formalized as the **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777, codex-validated 11-PR plan). Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` ΓÇö even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2nΓêÆ1ΓêÆx` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` ΓÇö reflection map `╬╕(A) ╧â ╧ä = conj(A(╧ü╧â)(╧ü╧ä))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `╬╕(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian ╬╕-decomposition ΓÇö orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (S┬╣/S┬│ real, S┬▓ imaginary), `╬╕(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)=┼£_{r x}┬╖┼£_{r y}`, `╬╕(├ö_L^(3))=ΓêÆ├ö_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `╬╕(─ñ)=─ñ` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `╬╕(─ñ_h)=─ñ_{ΓêÆh}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) Γèù I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `╬╕` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 Γëñ Re ╧å(╬╕(A)┬╖A)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `╬▓ = 0` base case ΓÇö `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X Γåª Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 Γëñ Re Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A) = Γêæ_{╧â,╬╝} ╬╕(A)╧â╬╝┬╖A╬╝╧â` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `╬╝=╧â`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A ╧â ╧â` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A) = conj S ┬╖ S = ΓÇûSΓÇû┬▓ ΓëÑ 0` with `S = Γêæ_Γäô D Γäô`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone ΓÇö `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `╬╕(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X Γåª Tr((╬╕(C)┬╖C)┬╖X)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((╬╕C┬╖C)┬╖(╬╕A┬╖A)) = Tr(╬╕(C┬╖A)┬╖(C┬╖A)) ΓëÑ 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` ΓÇö the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone ΓÇö `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X Γåª Tr(M┬╖X)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `Γêæ cß╡ó ╬╕(Cß╡ó)┬╖Cß╡ó`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow` (the product closure via `weightGen_mul`: `(╬╕C┬╖C)(╬╕D┬╖D) = ╬╕(CD)┬╖(CD)`), `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable Γƒ╣ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) ΓÇö the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(t┬╖Γêæcß╡ó╬╕(Cß╡ó)┬╖Cß╡ó)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums. **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` ΓÇö for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = t┬╖Γêæß╡ó cß╡ó ╬╕(Cß╡ó)┬╖Cß╡ó` (`t, cß╡ó ΓëÑ 0`, `Cß╡ó` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `Γêæ_{k<m}(k!)Γü╗┬╣Dß╡Å` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `LΓê₧`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology ΓÇö the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map ΓÇö `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported Γƒ╣ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`╬╕(exp X) = exp(╬╕ X)`: `╬╕` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) ΓÇö the building blocks for the `e^{-╬▓H_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `spinSDot_double_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the per-bond double commutator is the negated transverse bond energy `[┼£_a^{(3)}, [┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}]] = ΓêÆ(┼£_a^{(1)}┼£_b^{(1)} + ┼£_a^{(2)}┼£_b^{(2)})` (the f-sum-rule integrand / oscillator strength of the staggered mode), bounded by the bond energy | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commutator_onSiteS` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Heisenberg Hamiltonian commutator distributes over bonds `[─ñ_J, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = ╬ú_x ╬ú_y J_{xy} [┼£_x┬╖┼£_y, ┼£_z^{(3)}]`; with the per-bond case analysis this reduces the spin-current divergence `[─ñ, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_right` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}] = i(┼£_a^{(1)} ┼£_b^{(2)} ΓêÆ ┼£_a^{(2)} ┼£_b^{(1)})` for `a Γëá b` (only the transverse bond components fail to commute with `┼£_a^{(3)}`, via the cyclic SU(2) commutators; `_right` is the symmetric right-endpoint companion `[┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_b^{(3)}]`, and `_eq_zero_of_ne` is the off-bond vanishing `[┼£_x┬╖┼£_y, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = 0` for `z Γêë {x,y}`); the building block of `[─ñ, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` and the double commutator in the infrared / f-sum-rule bound | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state quadratic-form expansion `Γƒ¿╬ª, ├ö┬▓ ╬ªΓƒ⌐ = ╬ú_x ╬ú_y ╬╡_x ╬╡_y Γƒ¿╬ª, ┼£_x^{(3)} ┼£_y^{(3)} ╬ªΓƒ⌐`, rewriting the order parameter in the exact form `star ╬ª Γ¼¥ß╡Ñ (├ö┬▓).mulVec ╬ª` of the Corollary through the two-point functions the RP machinery bounds | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_eq_sum` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquare.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator squared expands as the two-point double sum `├ö┬▓ = ╬ú_x ╬ú_y ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ┼£_x^{(3)} ┼£_y^{(3)}` ΓÇö the bridge from the order parameter to the two-point functions bounded by the reflection-positivity / infrared machinery (the squared order parameter per `L┬▓` is controlled by the total two-point sum, only `O(L)` in one dimension) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquare.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz_abs` | **RP infra layer 40** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the square-root (usable) form of the RP CauchyΓÇôSchwarz ΓÇö `|Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)| Γëñ ΓêÜ(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A))┬╖ΓêÜ(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B))` for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` and left-supported `A,B`, by taking roots in the chessboard inequality (diagonals nonnegative); the form iterated in the DLS spreading argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean` |
| `ringTranslateConj` / `chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of` | **RP infra layer 39** (`RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the ring translation `╧ä(X) = TΓÇá┬╖X┬╖T` is a trace-preserving `*`-homomorphism (`ringTranslateConj_mul`/`_trace`), so any chessboard estimate transports unchanged to the translation-conjugated data `(╧äM, ╧ä(╬╕A), ╧äB)` (`chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of`) ΓÇö the weight `╧äM` is the Marshall gauge relative to the shifted reflection plane; carries the single-axis chessboard to every bond toward Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_gibbs` / `thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation` | **RP infra layer 38** (`RingTranslationGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): unlike the half-system gauge, the physical ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so the physical Gibbs operator `exp(ΓêÆ╬▓┬╖─ñ)` is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_gibbs`) and every physical thermal average is translation covariant `Γƒ¿TΓÇáATΓƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐` (`thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation`); the thermal-state translation symmetry for the momentum-space infrared analysis | `Quantum/SpinS/RingTranslationGibbs.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_ringReflectionThetaS_onSiteS` / `_spinSDot` | **RP infra layer 37** (`RingReflectionTranslation.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): translation covariance of the fixed-axis reflection map `╬╕` ΓÇö conjugating `╬╕(onSiteS x A)` (resp. `╬╕(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)`) by the ring translation `chainTranslationOp` shifts the reflection axis, sending the sites to `finRotate (ringReflect x)` (resp. both endpoints); the foundation for reflections across every bond, needed to spread the single-axis chessboard toward DLS Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTranslation.lean` |
| `ringDLSGibbs_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 36** (`RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete chessboard inequality for the gauged ring Gibbs weight ΓÇö combining its reflection positivity (Gibbs capstone) and reflection invariance (`╬╕M=M`), for `╬▓ ΓëÑ 0` and left-supported `A,B`, `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B))┬▓ Γëñ Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` with `M = exp(ΓêÆ╬▓┬╖toHamiltonian)`; the DLS factorization estimate for the thermal state of the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_toHamiltonian` / `_ringDLSGibbs` | **RP infra layer 35** (`RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauged DLS Hamiltonian is reflection-invariant `╬╕(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `H_L + ╬╕(H_L)` part is swapped to itself; each crossing bond `╬╕(crossBondInteractionS x) = crossBondInteractionS x` since `┼£_x^╬▒` commutes with its right-half reflection `╬╕(┼£_x^╬▒)`), hence the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(ΓêÆ╬▓┬╖toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant ΓÇö supplying the `╬╕M = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal lemmas | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean` |
| `rpForm_re_symm` / `rpTraceWeight_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 34** (`RingReflectionChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` (`╬╕M=M`) and left-supported `A,B`, the off-diagonal form value is symmetric `Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B) = Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖A)` (since `A` commutes with the right-half `╬╕B`); feeding this into the CauchyΓÇôSchwarz discriminant collapses the cross term, giving the chessboard bound `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B))┬▓ Γëñ Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` ΓÇö the DLS factorization step underlying Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_trace` / `rpForm_diag_im_zero` | **RP infra layer 33** (`RingReflectionTraceReality.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection map conjugates the trace `Tr(╬╕X) = conj(Tr X)` (reindex the diagonal by the involutive site reflection), so for a reflection-invariant weight `M` (`╬╕M = M`) and left-supported `A` the RP-form diagonal `Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)` is real (`╬╕┬▓=id`, `*`-homomorphism, `A` commutes with `╬╕A`); together with the CauchyΓÇôSchwarz this makes `(A,B)ΓåªTr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)` a genuine positive-semidefinite form with real diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceReality.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz` | **RP infra layer 32** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): a reflection-positive trace weight `M` makes `(A,B) Γåª Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)` a positive-semidefinite sesquilinear form on the left subalgebra; expanding `0 Γëñ Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕(AΓêÆtB)┬╖(AΓêÆtB))` as a nonnegative real quadratic in `t` and taking its discriminant (`discrim_le_zero`) gives `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B) + Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖A))┬▓ Γëñ 4┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` ΓÇö the workhorse for the DLS infrared bound | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `thermalAverageReS_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 31** (`RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge is a similarity transform, so it leaves the partition function invariant (`thermalPartitionFnS_toHamiltonian_eq`) and transports thermal averages ΓÇö the physical ring Heisenberg canonical average of `A` equals the reflection-positive DLS canonical average of `rightGauge ┬╖ A ┬╖ rightGaugeInv`; the bridge carrying RP bounds back to the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean` |
| `exp_neg_smul_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 30** (`RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the right-half gauge commutes with the matrix exponential (`Matrix.exp_units_conj`), so the physical ring Heisenberg Gibbs operator `exp(ΓêÆ╬▓ ─ñ)` and the reflection-positive DLS Gibbs operator `exp(ΓêÆ╬▓ ┬╖ ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian)` are unitarily equivalent ΓÇö physical thermal averages become DLS thermal averages of gauge-transformed observables | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 29** (`RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge conjugates the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian into the reflection-positive DLS Hamiltonian ΓÇö `rightGauge ┬╖ heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) ┬╖ rightGaugeInv = ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian` (bond Hamiltonians fixed, the two crossing bonds become the negated gauged crossing interactions); the unitary equivalence bridging the physical ring antiferromagnet to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian` / `_theta_*` | **RP infra layer 28** (`RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): summing the bond-invariance lemmas, the right-half gauge fixes both the left bond Hamiltonian (`rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian`) and the reflected right bond Hamiltonian `╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`rightGauge_conj_theta_ringLeftHamiltonian`) ΓÇö only the two crossing bonds are affected | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_crossBond` | **RP infra layer 27** (`RingReflectionCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauge image of a crossing bond is the negated gauged crossing interaction. For a left site `x < n` (reflection `r x` a right site), `rightGauge ┬╖ (┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_{r x}) ┬╖ rightGaugeInv = ΓêÆcrossBondInteractionS x` ΓÇö the left endpoint untouched, the right endpoint gauged (flipping `┼£^1`/`┼£^3` signs), matching `crossBondInteractionS_eq`. Both ring crossing bonds `(nΓêÆ1,n)` and `(2nΓêÆ1,0)` are of this form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left`/`_right` | **RP infra layer 26** (`RingReflectionBondConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge fixes every non-crossing bond. `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left` (left bond, both sites `< n`: untouched) and `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_right` (right bond, both sites gauged: the `┼£^1`/`┼£^3` sign flips cancel in the product, `┼£^2` unchanged). Only the two crossing bonds acquire the DLS sign flip. Helpers `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS_left`/`_right` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_mul` (gauge homomorphism) | **RP infra layer 25** (`RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation `X Γåª rightGauge┬╖X┬╖rightGaugeInv` by the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge is an algebra homomorphism ΓÇö `rightGauge_conj_mul` (over products, via `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge`), `rightGauge_conj_add`, `rightGauge_conj_smul`, `rightGauge_conj_sum`. With `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` these compute the gauge image of any operator built from single-site operators (the ring Hamiltonian in particular) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean` |
| `AxisTwoPiRotS` / `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` | **RP infra layer 24** (`RingReflectionGauge.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge. `manyBodyTensorS_conj_onSiteS_dep` ΓÇö site-dependent tensor conjugation `(Γèù_i W_i)(onSiteS z A)(Γèù_i Winv_i) = onSiteS z (W_z┬╖A┬╖Winv_z)` (for `W_i┬╖Winv_i = 1`). `AxisTwoPiRotS` ΓÇö the single-site ╧Ç-rotation around axis 2 as an interface (following `AxisSwapUnitaryS`; fields `U ┼£^1 UΓü╗┬╣ = ΓêÆ┼£^1`, `U ┼£^2 UΓü╗┬╣ = ┼£^2`, `U ┼£^3 UΓü╗┬╣ = ΓêÆ┼£^3`, non-vacuous at spin-1/2). `rightGauge`/`rightGaugeInv` ΓÇö the gauge unitary applying `U` on the right sites only; `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge` (invertible) and `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` (conjugation acts as `U┬╖A┬╖UΓü╗┬╣` on right sites, trivially on left). The interface is made **non-vacuous at spin-1/2** by `axisTwoPiRotSpinHalf` (the explicit `spinHalfRot2 ╧Ç`). The gauge bridging the ungauged ring Hamiltonian to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGauge.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_ungauged_dls` | **RP infra layer 23** (`RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ungauged DLS split of the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Combining the bond split (#4798) with `ringRightBondSum = ╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (#4799): `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + ╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian) + (┼£_{nΓêÆ1}┬╖┼£_n + ┼£_{2nΓêÆ1}┬╖┼£_0)`. The crossing term is the *ungauged* AFM crossing bond, differing from the gauged `crossBondInteractionS` (with `┼£┬▓` sign flipped) by the DLS/Marshall gauge; bridging the two via the gauge unitary is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean` |
| `ringRightBondSum_eq_theta` | **RP infra layer 22** (`RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right bond Hamiltonian equals the reflection of the left bond Hamiltonian. Reindexing by the bijection `x Γåª r(x+1)` (reflection of the cyclic successor `ringBondSucc`, shown bijective `= ┬╖+1` via `Equiv.addRight`) and using `spinSDot` symmetry, `ringRightBondSum = ╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`Fintype.sum_bijective`). This identifies the right part of the bond split with the `╬╕(H_L)` term of the DLS decomposition; combined with the bond split (#4798), `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + ╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian) + (ungauged crossing bonds)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_bond_split` | **RP infra layer 21** (`RingReflectionBondSplit.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian split into left, right, and crossing bonds. The bonds `(x, x+1)` of `Fin (2n)` fall into four disjoint classes by `x.val` (left `x Γëñ nΓêÆ2`, crossing `x = nΓêÆ1`, right `n Γëñ x Γëñ 2nΓêÆ2`, crossing `x = 2nΓêÆ1`); summing the bond form over this partition (`bondTerm_four_way` per-bond decomposition + `Finset.sum_add_distrib` + `sum_ite_val_eq`) gives `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + ringRightBondSum + ┼£_{nΓêÆ1}┬╖┼£_n + ┼£_{2nΓêÆ1}┬╖┼£_0`. Identifying `ringRightBondSum` with `╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` and the crossing bonds with the gauged interaction is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSplit.lean` |
| `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` | **RP infra layer 20** (`RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete gauged ring antiferromagnet has a reflection-positive Gibbs weight. Instantiating the ring DLS decomposition with the concrete left part `H_L = ringLeftHamiltonian` (`ringDLSDecomposition`), `ringDLSDecomposition_toHamiltonian` gives the gauged ring Hamiltonian `H = ringLeftHamiltonian + ╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian) ΓêÆ (crossBondInteractionS 0 + crossBondInteractionS (nΓêÆ1))`, and `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` concludes (via the Gibbs capstone) that `exp(ΓêÆ╬▓┬╖H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight (`╬▓ ΓëÑ 0`). | `ringReflectionThetaS_ringLeftHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 19** (`RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflected left bond Hamiltonian is the right bond Hamiltonian. This is the right part `╬╕(H_L)` of the ring DLS decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_eq_leftBondSum` | **RP infra layer 18** (`RingReflectionLeftBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left-half bond Hamiltonian as a sum over left bonds. | `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum` | **RP infra layer 17** (`RingReflectionBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian as a sum over nearest-neighbor bonds. This bond form is the starting point for the left/right/crossing bond split | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **RP infra layer 16** (`RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left part `H_L` of the ring DLS decomposition is left-supported. `SupportedOnLeftS.sum` (left-supportedness closed under finite sums); `heisenbergHamiltonianS_supportedOnLeft` (a Heisenberg Hamiltonian whose coupling `J x y` vanishes unless both sites are in the left half is left-supported, being `Γêæ_{x,y} J x y ┬╖ ┼£_x┬╖┼£_y` with each surviving term left-supported); `ringLeftCoupling` (the ring nearest-neighbor coupling restricted to left-half sites); and `ringLeftHamiltonian = heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringLeftCoupling n) N`, the concrete `H_L` that feeds `ringCrossingRPDecomposition`. | `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (ring instance) | **RP infra layer 15** (`RingReflectionRingInstance.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition of the gauged ring antiferromagnet. | `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (**Gibbs RP capstone**) | **RP infra layer 14** (`RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): **full Gibbs reflection positivity** ΓÇö for a reflection-positive decomposition `H = H_L + ╬╕(H_L) ΓêÆ D` (`RPDecomposition`) and `╬▓ ΓëÑ 0`, the Gibbs weight `exp(ΓêÆ╬▓┬╖H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight. The DysonΓÇôLiebΓÇôSimon Trotter assembly: `e^{ΓêÆ╬▓H} = e^{A+B}` (`A = ΓêÆ╬▓┬╖(H_L+╬╕H_L)`, `B = ╬▓┬╖D`), via the Lie product formula `e^{A+B} = lim_m (e^{A/m}┬╖e^{B/m})^m` (`LieProduct.lieProductFormula`); each factor is consumed by an accumulating RP trace weight ΓÇö the kinetic factor `e^{A/m} = exp(Y+╬╕Y)` is cone-representable (`coneRep_exp_add` + `mul_coneRep_right`) and the interaction factor `e^{B/m}` is the exp of a cone-representable operator (`mul_exp_coneRep_right`) ΓÇö then induction over the power and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`. Helpers: `realSmul_add_theta`, `RPDecomposition.interaction_coneRep`. This is the reflection positivity at the heart of the DLS / Shastry argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_exp_coneRep_right` | **RP infra layer 13** (`RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): an RP trace weight absorbs the exponential of a cone-representable operator. If `M` is RP and `P` is `RPTraceConeRepS`, then `M ┬╖ exp P` is RP ΓÇö the partial sums `Γêæ_{k<m}(k!)Γü╗┬╣Pß╡Å` are cone-representable (`pow`/`smul_nonneg`/`add`), so each `M┬╖(partial sum)` is RP (`mul_coneRep_right`), and `M┬╖exp P` is their limit (continuity of left multiplication + `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`). This consumes the interaction Gibbs factor `e^{╬▓D/m}` in the Trotter expansion | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean` |
| `coneRep_exp_add` (kinetic factor) | **RP infra layer 12** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the kinetic Gibbs factor is cone-representable. For left-supported `X`, `exp(X + ╬╕ X) = exp X ┬╖ ╬╕(exp X) = ╬╕(exp X) ┬╖ exp X` (`ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq`, via `exp_add_of_commute` since `X` and `╬╕(X)` commute, `╬╕(exp X) = exp(╬╕ X)`, and `mul_theta_comm`); the right-hand side is a single cone generator `╬╕(L)┬╖L` (`L = exp X` left-supported), so `coneRep_exp_add` gives `RPTraceConeRepS (exp(X + ╬╕ X))`. This is the kinetic building block (`e^{ΓêÆ╬▓HΓéÇ/m}`) the Trotter expansion of `e^{ΓêÆ╬▓H}` consumes | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right` (cone closure) | **RP infra layer 11** (`RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection-positive trace weights form a convex cone ΓÇö `RPTraceWeightS.zero`/`one` (the identity is RP, the `╬▓=0` trace cone), `add`, `smul_nonneg`, `sum` (closed under finite sums) ΓÇö and `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right`: an RP trace weight times a cone-representable operator (`RPTraceConeRepS`) is again RP (since `M┬╖Γêæcß╡ó╬╕(Cß╡ó)Cß╡ó = Γêæcß╡ó(M┬╖╬╕(Cß╡ó)Cß╡ó)`, each RP by the cone-generator closure, summed). This is the algebraic engine that accumulates reflection positivity factor-by-factor in the Trotter expansion of `e^{ΓêÆ╬▓H}` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_weightGen_right` / `RPTraceWeightS.weightGen_mul_left` | **RP infra layer 10** (`RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): closure of reflection-positive trace weights under cone generators `╬╕(L)┬╖L` (`L` left-supported). The naive "conjugation" closure `M Γåª L┬╖M┬╖╬╕(L)` is **false** (the homomorphism order `╬╕(LA)=╬╕(L)╬╕(A)` clashes with the test operator `B=AL`, and left-supported operators do not commute); the correct closure multiplies by a cone generator: `M┬╖(╬╕(L)┬╖L)` is RP (substitute the test operator `A Γåª L┬╖A`, using `L┬╖╬╕(A)=╬╕(A)┬╖L` and `╬╕(L)┬╖╬╕(A)=╬╕(L┬╖A)`) and `(╬╕(L)┬╖L)┬╖M` is RP (substitute `A Γåª A┬╖L`, via trace cyclicity). These are the kinetic-factor building blocks of the full DysonΓÇôLiebΓÇôSimon Gibbs reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean` |
| `crossBondInteractionS` / `RPDecomposition` | **RP infra layer 9** (`RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean`, Tasaki ┬º4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition data and the crossing-bond interaction. `crossBondInteractionS x = ╬ú_╬▒ ┼£_x^╬▒┬╖╬╕(┼£_x^╬▒)` (left site `x`), with the **gauge identity** `crossBondInteractionS x = ┼£_x^1 ┼£_{r x}^1 ΓêÆ ┼£_x^2 ┼£_{r x}^2 + ┼£_x^3 ┼£_{r x}^3` (the lone `ΓêÆ` on the `┼£^2` term is the sign flipped by the DLS/Marshall gauge ΓÇö a ╧Ç-rotation around axis 2 on the right half ΓÇö making all three components contribute the same negative reflection-positive sign). Each `onSiteS x ┼£^╬▒` (`x<n`) is left-supported; `crossBondInteractionS_exp_rpTraceWeight` shows `exp(t┬╖crossBondInteractionS x)` (`tΓëÑ0`) is a reflection-positive trace weight (via `mul_theta_comm` + the interaction-exponential cone). The abstract `RPDecomposition` structure (`H_L + ╬╕(H_L) ΓêÆ ╬ú_b C_b┬╖╬╕(C_b)`, all `C_b` left-supported) packages the DLS form, with `interaction_exp_rpTraceWeight` giving the Gibbs interaction factor's reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d` | **Corollary 4.3** (┬º4.1, AXIOM; eq. (4.1.11)): absence of LRO in 1D. For the zero-field 1D AFM Heisenberg ring, the squared staggered order parameter per site vanishes in the thermodynamic limit `lim_{LΓåæΓê₧} Γƒ¿╬ª_GS\|(├ö_L^(3)/L)┬▓\|╬ª_GSΓƒ⌐ = 0` (╬╡ΓÇô╬┤ form). In the book a corollary of Thm 4.2 + Thm 3.2 + MLM; runs through the deferred double-limit form of Thm 3.2, so recorded as a documented axiom (in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean` |
| `staggeredCasimirOpS` / `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` | **Theorem 4.4** (┬º4.1, ShenΓÇôQiuΓÇôTian, PROVED axiom-free; eqs. (4.1.12)ΓÇô(4.1.13)): ferrimagnetic LRO on an asymmetric bipartite lattice. The SU(2)-invariant squared staggered operator `(├ö_╬¢)┬▓ = ╬ú_{x,y} ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ┼£_x┬╖┼£_y`, and the bound `S┬▓(\|A\|ΓêÆ\|B\|)┬▓ Γëñ Γƒ¿╬ª_GS\|(├ö_╬¢)┬▓\|╬ª_GSΓƒ⌐` for any normalized ground state of the connected bipartite AFM model (same hypotheses as Thm 2.3). **PROVED axiom-free** (Issues #4604/#4617 CLOSED; book proof = chain (4.1.16): (4.1.15) cross-term + LiebΓÇôMattis Thm 2.3 total spin + Casimir). PR1 (#4605) formalizes the **operator-algebra core** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROComponentAlgebra.lean`: `staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS` (transverse part of `(├ö_╬¢)┬▓`), `(├ö_╬¢)┬▓ = transverse + (├ö_╬¢^(3))┬▓`, the PSD drop `Γƒ¿transverseΓƒ⌐ Γëñ Γƒ¿(├ö_╬¢)┬▓Γƒ⌐`, `(┼£_tot^(╬▒))┬▓ = ╬ú_{x,y} ┼£_x^(╬▒)┼£_y^(╬▒)`, the Casimir identity `╬ú_{x,y}(┼£_x^(1)┼£_y^(1)+┼£_x^(2)┼£_y^(2)) = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ΓêÆ (┼£_tot^(3))┬▓`, and the M=0 reduction `Γƒ¿(┼£_tot)┬▓Γƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿transverseΓƒ⌐`. PR2 (#4606) adds the **cross-term inequality (4.1.15)** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTermCore.lean` (transverse correlation + ladder positivity + per-pair sign) + `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTerm.lean` (the summed cross-term inequality, split for build speed): the transverse correlation `Γƒ¿╬ª,T_xy ╬ªΓƒ⌐` (`T_xy = ┬╜(┼£_x^+┼£_y^- + ┼£_x^-┼£_y^+)`), its non-positivity for cross-sublattice pairs on a Marshall-positive sector ground state (`marshallSignS┬╖c`, `c>0`), and the summed dominance `Γƒ¿╬ª,(╬ú_{x,y}T_xy)╬ªΓƒ⌐.re Γëñ Γƒ¿╬ª,staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS ╬ªΓƒ⌐.re`. PR3 (#4607) adds the **total-spin value** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROTotalSpin.lean` (coupling-agnostic via a `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` hypothesis): `exists_centered_groundState_predictedCasimir_of_tasaki23` produces a centered (`┼£┬│_tot ╬ªΓü░=0`) Marshall ground state with `(┼£_tot)┬▓ ╬ªΓü░ = S_tot(S_tot+1)┬╖╬ªΓü░` (`S_tot=(\|A\|ΓêÆ\|B\|)N/2`), reusing `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_sector` + the extremal-Casimir machinery. PR4 (#4608) proves the **capstone** `ferrimagnetic_lro_completeBipartite_centered` in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCapstone.lean`: assembling chain (4.1.16) on the centered ground state, `(N/2)┬▓(\|A\|ΓêÆ\|B\|)┬▓┬╖Γƒ¿╬ªΓü░,╬ªΓü░Γƒ⌐.re Γëñ Γƒ¿╬ªΓü░,(├ö_╬¢)┬▓╬ªΓü░Γƒ⌐.re` (existence form, coupling-agnostic via `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3`; sorry-free, axiom-clean, independent of the `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom; that axiom is now itself proved in UniversalFinal). For build speed the proof's private Rayleigh-ratio helpers and the per-sector oriented bound `staggeredCasimir_weightComponent_bound_oriented` (now module-public: `S_tot┬▓┬╖ΓÇû╬ª_MΓÇû┬▓ Γëñ Γƒ¿╬ª_M,(├ö_╬¢)┬▓╬ª_MΓƒ⌐.re` for each magnetization weight component `╬ª_M` of a ground state) live in the companion `ΓÇªUniversalFinalCore.lean`, with the sum assembly + the public `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` kept in `ΓÇªUniversalFinal.lean`. | `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversalFinal.lean` + `ΓÇªUniversalFinalCore.lean` + `FerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `ΓÇªComponentAlgebra.lean` + `ΓÇªCrossTerm.lean` + `ΓÇªTotalSpin.lean` + `ΓÇªTotalSpinCore.lean` + `ΓÇªCapstone.lean` + `ΓÇªUniversal.lean` |
| `raiseLowerReachableS_of_connected` | **Connected-graph spin-config reachability** (┬º2.5, PROVED; Issue #4609, prereq for the connected MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis extension / Thm 4.4 axiom): on a connected graph `G`, any two equal-magnetization configs are raise/lower reachable ΓÇö `G.Connected ΓåÆ magSumS ╧â = magSumS ╧â' ΓåÆ RaiseLowerReachableS G ╧â ╧â'`. Discharged axiom-free by strong induction on the `configDistS` mismatch (surplus/deficit sites + connected-graph walk) with overflow-safe single-quantum path transport (`transportOne`; at each edge push the quantum forward when the next vertex has room, else recurse first to make room). Generalizes the complete-bipartite `raiseLowerReachableS_bipartiteCompleteGraph` to arbitrary connected graphs ΓÇö the combinatorial core of extending Theorem 2.3 to connected couplings (PR #4610). **PR2 (#4611)** lifts this to the dressed-matrix per-sector PerronΓÇôFrobenius positivity for a general connected bipartite `G` in `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedDressedPF.lean`: `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_connected` (`Γêâ k, 0 < (shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector A J N c M)^k ╧â' ╧â`) from `G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G : G.Adj x y ΓåÆ 0 < (J x y).re` (not complete-bipartite positivity), via the graph-agnostic edge-local witness `neg_dressedHeisenbergSReMatrix_apply_pos_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` + the connected reachability ΓÇö generalizing `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_bipartite`. **PR3 (#4612)** assembles **Theorem 2.3 itself for connected couplings**: `isIrreducible_shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector_connected` (ConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean) + 20 `_of_irreducible` chain variants parameterizing the PF-consuming MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis chain by the irreducibility result (graph-agnostic) + the capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_data_of_connected` (ConnectedTheorem23Core.lean, ConnectedTheorem23.lean): the per-magnetization-sector Marshall-positive ground states + global minimality of Theorem 2.3 hold for ANY connected bipartite `J` (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G`), not just complete-bipartite ΓÇö the `hOutside`/lower-bound side reuses `tasaki23_general_hOutside`/`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` (which need only `hJ_nn`). This is the connected-coupling extension of MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis (LiebΓÇôMattis 1962). **PR4 (#4613)** assembles the **connected-coupling ferrimagnetic LRO bound (existence form)** `ferrimagnetic_lro_connected_centered` (ConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean): for any connected bipartite AFM coupling (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + `hJ_pos_G`), there is a centered ground state `╬ªΓü░` with `(N/2)┬▓(\|A\|ΓêÆ\|B\|)┬▓┬╖Γƒ¿╬ªΓü░,╬ªΓü░Γƒ⌐.re Γëñ Γƒ¿╬ªΓü░,(├ö_╬¢)┬▓╬ªΓü░Γƒ⌐.re` ΓÇö Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) for the genuine connected (non-complete) coupling, built from the connected Theorem 2.3 data + connected irreducibility Casimir value + the PR1/PR2 operator-algebra (all graph-agnostic). **PR5 (#4614)** proves the **`SU(2)` invariance of `(├ö_╬¢)┬▓`** in `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean`: `staggeredCasimirOpS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` + `ΓÇªOpPlus`/`ΓÇªOpMinus` (the squared staggered order operator commutes with every total-spin operator), immediate from the per-pair `spinSDot_commutator_totalSpinSOp*` vanishing ΓÇö ingredient (a) of the universal-form transfer. **PR6 (#4615)** proves the **core of ingredient (b)** in `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean`: `su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_invariant` ΓÇö for an SU(2)-invariant operator `O` (commuting with `┼£^┬▒_tot`) and a joint `┼£┬│_tot`/Casimir eigenvector `v`, the real expectation ratio `Γƒ¿w,OwΓƒ⌐.re/Γƒ¿w,wΓƒ⌐.re` is unchanged under lowering `w Γåª ┼£Γü╗_tot v` (both numerator and denominator scale by the same `┼£Γü║┼£Γü╗ = Casimir ΓêÆ (┼£┬│)┬▓ + ┼£┬│` eigenvalue). This is the ladder step that makes `(├ö_╬¢)┬▓`'s expectation constant across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet; **PR7 (#4616)** iterates it to `(┼£Γü╗_tot)^k` (`su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_iterate_invariant`, `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean`), so every member of the lowering tower shares the centered ratio. The **stated** `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom (universal over *all* ground states) now remains pending only the final assembly of ingredient (b): the ground-state weight decomposition + the **ground-eigenspace classification** (any global-min eigenvector's nonzero `┼£┬│`-weight components lie in the admissible sectors ΓÇö needs strict energy separation, the hard remaining gate) (#4604). **PR8 (#4618)** adds step 1 of that dimension bound: `heisenbergHamiltonianSMatrixOnMagSector_finrank_le_one_of_marshall_positive_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean`) ΓÇö connected-coupling per-sector ground-state uniqueness (`finrank Γëñ 1`), the connected-irreducibility mirror of the complete-graph lemma (Issue #4617). **PR9 (#4619)** adds step 3: `weightPreserving_expectation_eq_sum_sector` (`Quantum/SpinS/WeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean`) ΓÇö for any operator commuting with `┼£┬│_tot`, `Γƒ¿╬ª,O╬ªΓƒ⌐ = ╬ú_M Γƒ¿╬ª_M,O╬ª_MΓƒ⌐` over the `┼£┬│`-weight sector components (cross terms vanish by `┼£┬│_tot`-eigenvalue orthogonality; no Hermiticity of `O` needed), the block-diagonal decomposition for `(├ö_╬¢)┬▓`'s expectation. **PR10 (#4620)** clears the hard gate (step 2): `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/StrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean`; for build speed the per-sector ferrimagnetic foundation `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_ferrimagnetic` and its Casimir-obstruction helpers live in the companion `ΓÇªStrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean`) ΓÇö **strict** energy separation, `╬╝ < ╬╝M` for any non-admissible sector `M Γêë [min┬╖N, max┬╖N]` (the connected ferrimagnetic generalization of the balanced `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_card_eq_zero_casimir_ladder_obstruction`, via the Casimir equality-obstruction: ladder a hypothetical non-admissible ╬╝-eigenvector to the admissible band edge, pull back the predicted Casimir `S_tot(S_tot+1)` through the ladder, contradict the abs Casimir lower bound `|centerΓêÆM|(|centerΓêÆM|+1) > S_tot(S_tot+1)`). This is the strict LiebΓÇôMattis level ordering for the connected AFM Heisenberg ΓÇö the last gate; only the final weight-decomposition assembly remains to discharge `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` (#4617). **PR11 (#4621)** adds the universal-form assembly infra in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean`: `chain_bound_marshall_sector` (Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) at an arbitrary-weight Marshall sector vector: `(╬│ ΓêÆ m┬▓)ΓÇûwΓÇû┬▓ Γëñ Γƒ¿w,(├ö_╬¢)┬▓wΓƒ⌐.re`), `star_dotProduct_self_eq_sum_sector` (`ΓÇû╬ªΓÇû┬▓ = ╬ú_M ΓÇû╬ª_MΓÇû┬▓`), `heisenbergHamiltonianS_magSectorProjection_eigen` (a weight component of an H-eigenvector is an H-eigenvector), the diagonal-shift `c` existence, and the global-flip / weight-commute helpers. The remaining step to remove the axiom is the SU(2) Rayleigh-ratio constancy across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet (transfer the per-sector bound from a near-central admissible sector to all sectors via the merged iterated ladder-invariance) (#4617) | `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedRaiseLower.lean` + `ΓÇªConnectedDressedPF.lean` + `ΓÇªConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean` + `ΓÇªConnectedTheorem23Core.lean` + `ΓÇªConnectedTheorem23.lean` + `ΓÇªConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `ΓÇªStaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean` + `ΓÇªSU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean` + `ΓÇªSU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean` + `ΓÇªConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean` + `ΓÇªWeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean` + `ΓÇªStrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean` + `ΓÇªStrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean` + `ΓÇªFerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean` |

(4.2.2)ΓÇô(4.2.4)): low-lying tower states. Builds the d-dim hypercubic torus model `Fin d ΓåÆ ZMod L` (`L^d` sites, NN coupling, parity sublattice) + staggered raising/lowering order operators `├ö_L^┬▒ = ╬ú_x ╬╡_x ┼£_x^┬▒`. The two-sided tower states `towerState M = (├ö_L^{sgn M})^{\|M\|} ╬ª_GS` (`M : Γäñ`, raising for MΓëÑ0 / lowering for M<0) satisfy `Γƒ¿╧ê_M,─ñ╧ê_MΓƒ⌐/Γƒ¿╧ê_M,╧ê_MΓƒ⌐ Γëñ E_GS + CΓééM┬▓/L^d` for `\|M\| Γëñ CΓéüL^{d/2}` (Rayleigh-ratio form, scale-invariant). **Conditional on LRO** (`qΓéÇ>0` premise: `Γƒ¿(├ö_L/L^d)┬▓Γƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿├ö┬▓Γƒ⌐/(Γƒ¿╬ª,╬ªΓƒ⌐┬╖(L^d)┬▓) ΓëÑ qΓéÇ`, eq. 4.1.7), so vacuous in d=1 per Cor 4.3. **Proof** (axiom-clean, `#print axioms` = propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound): the variational double-commutator bound (Γÿà, `tower_numerator_double_commutator_le`) reduces the gap to `Γƒ¿╬ª,[(├┤Γü╗)^M,[─ñ,(├┤Γü║)^M]]╬ªΓƒ⌐`; this numerator is bounded `Γëñ M┬▓(A/V┬╖P_M + B M┬▓/V┬▓┬╖P_M)` via Lemma R2 (split-independent renormalized commutator estimate `r2_split_independent`) + locality decay of `d╠é=[├┤Γü║,[─ñ,├┤Γü╗]]` and `[─ñ,├┤Γü║]` + moment-factorΓåÆ`P_M` conversions; the denominator `ΓÇû(├┤┬▒)^M ╬ªΓÇû┬▓ ΓëÑ ┬╜P_M` (Lemma R1, balanced-word `orderWord_balanced_re_close`); the LRO recursion `2qΓéÇP_n Γëñ P_{n+1}` (`phatMoment_succ_two_q0_le`) needs the **total-spin-singlet** premise `┼£┬│╬ª=┼£┬╣╬ª=0` (added to `IsAndersonTowerConstants`; faithful ΓÇö the bipartite AFM GS is the unique singlet by MLM Thm 2.3). `M<0` via Hermitian conjugation; constants `CΓéü=min(ΓêÜ(qΓéÇ/6N),ΓêÜ(2^d)ΓêÜ(2qΓéÇ)/16N)`, `CΓéé=288dNΓü┤/qΓéÇ+576CΓéü┬▓dN┬│(1+1/ΓêÜ(2qΓéÇ))/qΓéÇ`; `N=0` vacuous (order op = 0) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTower.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTheorem46.lean` |

(4.4.1)ΓÇô(4.4.6)): the 1D Heisenberg model is **disordered at any nonzero temperature**. New finite-temperature framework: Gibbs operator `thermalGibbsOpS ╬▓ H = e^{ΓêÆ╬▓H}`, partition `thermalPartitionFnS`, real canonical average `thermalAverageReS ╬▓ H A = Re Tr[A e^{ΓêÆ╬▓H}]/Re Tr[e^{ΓêÆ╬▓H}]` (eq. 4.4.3); field Hamiltonians `heisenbergFieldHamiltonianS ferro` covering ferro (4.4.1, uniform field) and AFM (4.4.2, staggered field); torus embedding `torusEmbed`, `Γäô┬╣` distance `intL1Dist`, even-side sequence `evenSide n=2(n+1)`. **(4.4.5)** `tasaki_4_22_magnetization_vanishes`: `lim_{hΓåô0} lim_{LΓåæΓê₧} Γƒ¿┼£_x^(3)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,h}^L=0` (no SSB; order of limits essential), stated soundly per footnote 41 (╬╡ΓÇô╬┤ with inner liminf-subsequence `ΓêânΓéÇ ΓêÇnΓëÑnΓéÇ ΓêâmΓëÑn, |mag|<╬╡`). **(4.4.6)** `tasaki_4_22_exponential_clustering`: Γêâ ╬╛(╬▓),C(╬▓)Γêê(0,Γê₧) with `|Γƒ¿┼£_x^(╬▒)┼£_y^(╬▒)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,0}^Γê₧| Γëñ C exp(ΓêÆ|xΓêÆy|/╬╛)`, bounding the concrete `liminf` correlation `infiniteVolSpinCorrLiminf` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |

Reuses the Theorem 4.22 finite-temperature framework at general `d` (`hd : 2 Γëñ d`): there exists `╬▓ΓéÇ Γêê (0,Γê₧)` (depending only on `d` and `S`, **not** on the ferro/AFM flag ΓÇö `ferro` is universally quantified under `Γêâ ╬▓ΓéÇ`) such that for every `╬▓ Γêê [0, ╬▓ΓéÇ]`, **(4.4.7)** the magnetization vanishes `lim_{hΓåô0} lim_{LΓåæΓê₧} Γƒ¿┼£_x^(3)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,h}^L = 0` (same ╬╡ΓÇô╬┤ / liminf-subsequence form) and **(4.4.8)** Γêâ ╬╛(╬▓),C(╬▓)Γêê(0,Γê₧) with `|Γƒ¿┼£_x^(╬▒)┼£_y^(╬▒)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,0}^Γê₧| Γëñ C exp(ΓêÆ|xΓêÆy|/╬╛)`. (4.4.21)ΓÇô(4.4.22)): the **improved HohenbergΓÇôMerminΓÇôWagner theorem** ΓÇö the 2D Heisenberg model exhibits no magnetic ordering of any type at any nonzero temperature. New `generalizedFieldHamiltonianS J ╬╛` (eq. 4.4.21): `J ╬ú ┼£┬╖┼£ ΓêÆ h ╬ú_x ╬ú_╬▒ (╬╛_x^╬▒) ┼£_x^(╬▒)` (field `h_x = h ╬╛_x`) with `J Γêê {ΓêÆ1,+1}` and an **arbitrary fixed field-direction family** `╬╛` (`\|╬╛_x\| Γëñ 1`), generalizing the uniform/staggered fields; `finiteVolMagnetizationGenS` is its finite-volume Gibbs magnetization `Γƒ¿┼£_x^(╬▒)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,h}^L`. For `d=2`, any `╬▓ Γêê [0,Γê₧)`, every `J=┬▒1`, every bounded `╬╛`, every component `╬▒=1,2,3`, and every `x Γêê Γäñ┬▓`: `lim_{hΓåô0} lim_{LΓåæΓê₧} Γƒ¿┼£_x^(╬▒)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,h}^L = 0` (4.4.22). The distinctness `0<\|xΓêÆy\|` is **required for soundness** (`Real.rpow 0 (ΓêÆ╬╖)=0` in Lean, so the bound would be false at `x=y` where the self-correlation is positive). `╬╖(╬▓) Γëâ (16CS┬▓╬▓)^{ΓêÆ1}` for large `╬▓`; the same `╬╖` is bound before `ferro` (works for both signs). New per-axis staggered order operator `staggeredOrderOpAxisS ╬▒ A N` (`╬▒=0/1/2 ΓåÆ ├ö^(1)/├ö^(2)/├ö^(3)`). For the AFM Heisenberg model with `d ΓëÑ 3` (`hd : 3 Γëñ d`) and any `S = N/2` with `N ΓëÑ 1` (`[NeZero N]`): there exist `╬▓ΓéÇ Γêê (0,Γê₧)` and `q(╬▓)` with `q(╬▓) > 0` for `╬▓ > ╬▓ΓéÇ` such that `Γƒ¿(├ö_L^(╬▒)/L^d)┬▓Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,0}^L = Γƒ¿(├ö_L^(╬▒))┬▓Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,0}^L / (L^d)┬▓ ΓëÑ q(╬▓)` for every `╬▒=1,2,3`, every `╬▓ > ╬▓ΓéÇ`, and all sufficiently large even volumes (the squared operator `├ö_L^(╬▒)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)┬▓` normalization ΓÇö the intensive LRO density, as in ┬º4.1/┬º4.2). `[NeZero N]` is **essential for soundness** (at `N=0` the order operator vanishes, contradicting `q(╬▓)>0`). Proof: reflection positivity (DLS; `d=3,S=1/2` via KennedyΓÇôLiebΓÇôShastry). The ferromagnetic LRO is only conjectured (RP fails) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |

(4.4.53)): the **Griffiths / KomaΓÇôTasaki theorem** ΓÇö under the same conditions as Theorem 4.26 (AFM, `d ΓëÑ 3`, `N ΓëÑ 1` `[NeZero N]`), low-temperature LRO is accompanied by genuine SSB. With the staggered field (eq. 4.4.2), `lim_{hΓåô0} lim_{LΓåæΓê₧} Γƒ¿├ö_L^(3)Γƒ⌐_{╬▓,h}^L / L^d ΓëÑ ΓêÜ(3 q(╬▓))` for `╬▓ > ╬▓ΓéÇ`. The axiom bundles the Theorem 4.26 LRO bound (4.4.52) and the SSB bound (4.4.53) under one **shared `Γêâ╬▓ΓéÇ Γêâq`**, making the "same q" dependence explicit; the `ΓêÜ3` has the same origin as Theorem 4.11. Double limit in ╬╡ΓÇô╬┤ form (outer `hΓåô0`, inner liminf as eventual lower bound). Proof: KomaΓÇôTasaki (extending Griffiths) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |

(5.1.1)ΓÇô(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. Hard-core bosons (`uΓåæΓê₧` limit of the bosonic Hubbard model, eq. 5.1.4) are **equivalent to the S=1/2 XY model** (eq. 5.1.5) via the identification `├ó_xΓÇáΓåö(ΓêÆ1)^x ┼£_x^+`, `n╠é_xΓåö┼£_x^(3)+┬╜`, `N╠éΓåö┼£_tot^(3)+L^d/2` (eqs. 5.1.6ΓÇô5.1.7); half filling `╧ü=┬╜` Γåö the `┼£_tot^(3)=0` sector. `xyHamiltonianS d L` = the XY Hamiltonian (XXZ at ╬╗=D=0, N=1). The bosonic order operators `├ö_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. (6.3.7)ΓÇô(6.3.9)): the **S=1 chain on the hidden-AFM subspace `H_HAF`**. **`tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` is now a proved theorem** (formerly a documented axiom; `#print axioms` ΓåÆ `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`): the final assembly `tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` bundles, for even `L>0`, a ground state `╬ª = hhafSubspaceEmbedding` of the balanced PerronΓÇôFrobenius eigenvector with (i) projection-fixedness + nonzero + eigenvector at `E = hhafMinEnergy`, (ii) minimality over `hhafRealSpectrum`, (iii) **uniqueness** (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_ground_finrank_le_one`: the restricted ground eigenspace is one-dimensional ΓÇö at `E < ΓêÆ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated since its eigenvalues are `ΓëÑ ΓêÆ2`, and the balanced block contributes the 1-D PF ground state via the injective restriction `R` into the balanced block eigenspace), lifted to the operator via `hhafSubspaceEmbedding_of_projFixed` + `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_of_projFixed_eig` + `finrank_le_one_iff`, (iv) the positive gap (`exists_hhaf_positive_gap`), and (v) exponential decay (`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`). For the spin-1 chain (`N=2`, configs `Fin L ΓåÆ Fin 3`, `╧â_x=0/1/2 Γåª +1/0/ΓêÆ1`), a config has **complete hidden AFM order** (`IsHiddenAFMConfig`, via `IsPM`/`InCyclicOpen`/`IsNextPM`) when the nonzero (`┬▒`) spins strictly alternate around the ring with arbitrary `0`-spins between (eq. 6.3.9). `H_HAF` = span of those basis states; `hhafProjection` (diagonal projection), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS = P_HAF┬╖─ñ┬╖P_HAF`, `hhafRealSpectrum` (eigenvalues with `H_HAF` eigenvectors). For even `L>0`, the restricted chain has a **unique ground state**, a **finite gap** (`gap>0` in the `H_HAF`-restricted spectrum), and **exponentially decaying** correlation `\|chainCorrelation\| Γëñ C e^{ΓêÆd(x,y)/╬╛}` (`ringDist`) ΓÇö so the Haldane conjecture for `S=1` holds rigorously within this artificial subspace. Proof: path-integral / 2D classical stat-mech. **Spectral foundations now proved** (infrastructure for the now-proved theorem, Issue #4718): `hhafProjection_isHermitian` (P_HAF Hermitian), `hhafProjection_mul_self` (P_HAF idempotent), `afmHeisenbergChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian`, `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (compressed `P_HAF┬╖─ñ┬╖P_HAF` Hermitian). **Ground-energy existence now proved**: `hhafConfig` (hidden-AFM config subtype), `hhafRestrictedMatrix` (H_HAF├ùH_HAF submatrix, Hermitian), `hhafSubspaceEmbedding` (zero-extension) with the compressionΓÇôembedding intertwining `P_HAF┬╖─ñ┬╖P_HAF Γêÿ emb = emb Γêÿ restMat`, and `hhafMinEnergy`/`hhafMinEnergy_mem_realSpectrum` (the minimal restricted eigenvalue is a genuine `hhafRealSpectrum` member with a nonzero H_HAF ground state), and `exists_hhaf_min_real_eigenvalue` (it is `Γëñ` every restricted eigenvalue, so a genuine restricted **ground energy** with minimality). **Per-L exp-decay** proved (`exp_decay_envelope_of_finite`/`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`): on the finite ring any state's correlation admits `|Γƒ¿┼£_x┬╖┼£_yΓƒ⌐| Γëñ C e^{ΓêÆd/╬╛}` (╬╛=1, C=╬ú|corr|e^{d}) ΓÇö the decay clause of Prop 6.5 (now all clauses proved). **Max-energy infra** added: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eigenvalue_mem_realSpectrum` (every restricted-matrix eigenvalue embeds into `hhafRealSpectrum`), `hhafMaxEnergy` + `hhafMaxEnergy_mem_realSpectrum`; **spectral completeness/finiteness** (`hhafRealSpectrum_restrict` reverse bridge, `hhafRealSpectrum_subset_eigenvalue_range` via mathlib `spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`, `hhafRealSpectrum_finite`) ΓÇö the restricted spectrum IS the finite restricted-matrix eigenvalue set. **Diagonal bounds**: new general `HermitianVariationalUpperBound` (max-Rayleigh) + `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag`/`diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` give `hhafMinEnergy Γëñ (restMat ╧â ╧â).re Γëñ hhafMaxEnergy` via Rayleigh at the basis vector (toward non-scalarity for the gap). **Non-scalarity PROVED**: `hhafMinEnergy_lt_hhafMaxEnergy` (even LΓëÑ2) via all-zero config (diag=0) and domain-wall config ╧â=[0,2,1,ΓÇª,1] (HAF, diag<0); general formula `hhaf_diag_eq_succ_sum` (diag = ╬ú_x (1-╧âx)(1-╧â_{x+1})). **Positive gap PROVED**: `exists_hhaf_positive_gap` (even L>0) ΓÇö first-excited eigenvalue EΓéü = (eigenvalues image filtered above min).min with EΓéü Γêê spectrum, EΓéü > minEig (gap>0), and minimal among spectrum elements above minEig (via completeness). These foundations feed the now-discharged uniqueness clause. **PF real-form foundation** started: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_im_zero` (entries real), `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal` (real symmetric form) + `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal_ofReal`/`_isSymm` ΓÇö the entry point for the Perron-Frobenius uniqueness route (Marshall gauge (now built: `ringSublattice`, `hhafDressedMatrix`, `hhafDressedMatrix_eq` = ╬╡(╧â)╬╡(╧ä)┬╖M_real via the dressed-Heisenberg machinery) + **symmetrized-coupling infra** for the off-diagonal sign step (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_coupling_swap`: the Heisenberg Hamiltonian is invariant under transposing the coupling, via `spinSDot_comm`; `ringCouplingSym` = `ringCoupling + ringCouplingß╡Ç` with `_symm`/`_im_zero`/`_re_nonneg`; `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCouplingSym`: `H(ringCouplingSym) = 2┬╖H(ringCoupling)`, so the directed ring Hamiltonian equals ┬╜ the symmetric-coupling one ΓÇö letting the symmetric-coupling off-diagonal nonpositivity lemma apply) ΓÇö **off-diagonal nonpositivity now PROVED**: `ringCouplingSym_bipartite` (even ring `L` is bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice`: for even `L`, `(a+1)%L` flips parity, so equal-parity sites are never adjacent ΓÇö via `Nat.mod_mod_of_dvd`), `dressedHeisenbergS_ringCoupling_re_eq_half` (dressed directed = ┬╜ dressed symmetric), and `hhafDressedMatrix_offdiag_nonpos` (even `L`: `hhafDressedMatrix ╧â ╧ä Γëñ 0` for `╧â Γëá ╧ä`, via the symmetric-coupling nonpositivity lemma) ΓÇö the diagonal `┬▒1` Marshall gauge makes the AFM off-diagonals sign-definite. **Symmetry + nonneg shift now PROVED**: `hhafDressedMatrix_isSymm` (the dressed matrix is symmetric), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_eq` (diagonal = real restricted diagonal, since the Marshall sign squares to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` (diagonal Γëñ max energy), and `hhafShifted_entry_nonneg` (for `c ΓëÑ hhafMaxEnergy` and even `L`, the shifted matrix `c┬╖I ΓêÆ M` has nonnegative entries ΓÇö the nonnegativity hypothesis of `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric`). **Irreducibility started** (Issue #4732, G├│mez-Santos kink ergodicity): `hhafShifted_pos_of_ladderStep` ΓÇö two hidden-AFM configs differing by one adjacent raise/lower (ladder) move on a bipartite bond have a strictly positive shifted entry `(c┬╖I ΓêÆ M) ╧ä ╧â > 0` (reusing `dressedHeisenbergS_apply_re_neg_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` on `ringCouplingSym` + the ┬╜ scaling), the single-edge positivity for the PerronΓÇôFrobenius reachability argument. **Ring bond graph** added: `hhafRingGraph` (cyclic nearest-neighbour graph on `Fin L`), with `hhafRingGraph_adj_sublattice_ne` (bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice` for even `L`) and `ringCouplingSym_re_pos_of_ringGraph_adj` (the symmetrized coupling is strictly positive on every edge) ΓÇö the bond-graph data feeding the per-step positivity. **HAF reachability + walkΓåÆpower** added: `RaiseLowerStepSHhaf` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf` (single HAF-preserving ladder move / its reflexive-transitive closure), `hhafShifted_pos_of_stepHhaf` (each step gives a strictly positive shifted entry), and `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable` (HAF-reachability ΓçÆ some power `(c┬╖I ΓêÆ M)^k` is strictly positive ΓÇö the walkΓåÆpower bridge, mirroring the magnetization-sector version). **Kink-reduction setup** added: `hhafCanonical` (the all-`0`-spin configuration, base case), `pmCount` (number of `┬▒` spins, the induction measure), `pmCount_eq_zero_iff` (no `┬▒` spins Γƒ║ canonical). **Slide move** added: `slidePM` (move the spin at `a` onto a cyclic neighbour `b`, leaving a `0` at `a`), with `slidePM_apply` (its pointwise values) and `slidePM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single raise/lower ladder step on the ring-graph bond `{a, b}` when `a` carries a `┬▒` spin and `b` carries a `0`). **Slide preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity core): `slidePM_isHiddenAFM` ΓÇö sliding a `┬▒` spin onto its `0`-carrying cyclic successor keeps the strict `+,ΓêÆ,ΓÇª` alternation, proved by transferring each `IsNextPM` pair of the moved configuration back to one of `╧â` (relabelling `b Γåª a`) via the open-arc decomposition lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_left_imp` / `inCyclicOpen_succ_right_imp` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ` (each an `omega` calculation after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), `annihPM_apply`, and `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single ladder step when `a, b` carry opposite `┬▒` spins). **Annihilation preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN**: `annihPM_isHiddenAFM` ΓÇö removing an adjacent opposite-sign `+,ΓêÆ` pair keeps the alternation (each `IsNextPM` pair of the annihilated config either avoids the removed pair, giving an `IsNextPM` pair of `╧â`, or straddles it, where the chain `x, a, b, y` forces opposite endpoint signs), via the sub-arc lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_iff_mem` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_left` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `┬▒` count), `annihPM_pmCount_card_lt` (annihilation strictly decreases it). **CONNECTIVITY PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity theorem): the reduction measure `hhafS = ╬ú_{┬▒ spins}(L ΓêÆ x)` strictly decreases under a slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder move to another balanced config of smaller measure (first `┬▒` spin at `p Γëñ LΓêÆ2`, sliding past a `0` or annihilating an adjacent opposite `┬▒`); `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical` / `hhaf_reachable_canonical` (strong induction on `hhafS`) then show **every balanced hidden-AFM configuration is HAF-reachable from the canonical all-`0` configuration** (with `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible ΓÇö a single-`┬▒` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `┬▒1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. **Balanced-sector block irreducibility PROVEN**: `hhafConfig0` (the even-`pmCount` subtype) with `hhafShiftedMatrix0` (the `c┬╖I ΓêÆ M` block), `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical0` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walkΓåÆpower), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` ΓÇö for even `L` and shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`, the balanced-sector shifted matrix is PerronΓÇôFrobenius irreducible (`isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: nonnegative + entrywise-positive power, with the diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = c┬╖I ΓêÆ MΓéÇ`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` ΓÇö applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `┬▒1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`MΓéÇ = ╬ÿ┬╖M_realΓéÇ┬╖╬ÿ`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` ΓÇö transferring the **full PerronΓÇôFrobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `╬ÿv`, ground minimality, and `finrank Γëñ 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `Γäé` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis statement within `H_HAF`). **N├⌐el witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `ΓêÆ1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring ΓÇö all sites are `┬▒`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`┬▒`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the N├⌐el configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy Γëñ ΓêÆL PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the N├⌐el diagonal energy is `ΓêÆL`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(ΓêÆ1) = ΓêÆ1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy Γëñ ΓêÆL`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the N├⌐el state) ΓÇö the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `ΓêÆL`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`┬▒1` single-`┬▒` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` ΓÇö a single-`┬▒` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `┬▒`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` ΓÇö the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|Γƒ¿╧â'|┼£_x┬╖┼£_y|╧âΓƒ⌐| Γëñ 1` (each `ΓêÜ` factor `Γëñ ΓêÜ2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` ΓÇö for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M ╧â ╧ä| Γëñ (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `┬▒1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym ┬╖ spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `Γëñ 1`). **Magnetization-`┬▒1` sector min `ΓëÑ ΓêÆ2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`┬▒` `Sz=┬▒1` (`pmCount = 1`) ΓÇö there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector LiebΓÇôMattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `┬▒` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `╧ä` to be the slide `slidePM` of `╧â`'s `┬▒` spin to its new site, sharing the `┬▒` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `Γëñ ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `┬▒`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`Γêæ_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` Γƒ╣ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `Γëñ 2`: every single-`┬▒` eigenvalue `ΓëÑ ΓêÆ2`) ΓÇö the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = ┬▒1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`Γëñ ΓêÆL Γëñ ΓêÆ2`). **Balanced ground energy `< ΓêÆL` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the PerronΓÇôFrobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the N├⌐el row gives `╬╝┬╖v(N├⌐el) = ΓêÆL┬╖v(N├⌐el) + ╬ú_{╧äΓëáN├⌐el} MΓéÇ(N├⌐el,╧ä) v(╧ä)`; every off-diagonal term is `Γëñ 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`┬▒` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`MΓéÇ(N├⌐el,N├⌐el) = ΓêÆL`) we get `╬╝┬╖v(N├⌐el) < ΓêÆL┬╖v(N├⌐el)`, hence `╬╝ < ΓêÆL`. Since `ΓêÆL Γëñ ΓêÆ2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** ΓÇö the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` ΓÇö an odd count `ΓëÑ 3` is impossible. Proof: the `┬▒` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` ΓÇö no `┬▒` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `┬▒` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `┬▒` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - ┬╖` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `┬▒` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` ΓÇö the cyclic wrap arc holds no `┬▒` spin), which would force the first and last `┬▒` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count ΓÇö contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount Γƒ╣ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`┬▒` Γƒ╣ `magSumS = L ┬▒ 1 Γëá L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`┬▒` `= Sz ┬▒1`, with no higher sectors ΓÇö the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`┬▒` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish ΓÇö magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{╧â // P ╧â}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`┬▒` config couples only to single-`┬▒`, an even config only to even) ΓÇö the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`┬▒` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank Γëñ 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `Γäé` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` ΓÇö the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realΓåÆcomplex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced PerronΓÇôFrobenius `finrank Γëñ 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` ΓÇö the **complex single-`┬▒` block has all eigenvalues `ΓëÑ ΓêÆ2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `ΓëÑ ΓêÆ2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < ΓêÆ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) ΓÇö the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced PerronΓÇôFrobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The PerronΓÇôFrobenius uniqueness development ΓÇö kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`┬▒1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy ΓÇö lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

### The AKLT model (Tasaki ┬º7.1)

| `akltHamiltonianS` / `aklt_theorem_7_1` | **┬º7.1.1 Theorem 7.1** (the AKLT main theorem; AXIOM; eqs. (7.1.1)ΓÇô(7.1.2)): the AffleckΓÇôKennedyΓÇôLiebΓÇôTasaki `S=1` chain. `akltHamiltonianS L = ╬ú_x {┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1} + Γàô(┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1})┬▓}` (eq. 7.1.1, PBC ring, SU(2)-invariant; the biquadratic term puts it outside MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis). For all sufficiently large chains (`L = n+1`, any parity), `aklt_theorem_7_1` gives: a **unique ground state** `╬ª` (= the valence-bond-solid VBS state; nonzero eigenvector at `EΓéÇ`, every ground eigenvector Γê¥ ╬ª); a **spectral gap** `ΓëÑ ╬öEΓéÇ` for a positive `L`-independent `╬öEΓéÇ` (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`); and the **exact correlation** `Γƒ¿╬ª,┼£_x┬╖┼£_y ╬ªΓƒ⌐/Γƒ¿╬ª,╬ªΓƒ⌐ ΓåÆ 4(ΓêÆ3)^{ΓêÆ\|xΓêÆy\|}` (eq. 7.1.2, sign-alternating exponential decay) for fixed sites with `\|xΓêÆy\| ΓëÑ 1`, as `LΓåæΓê₧` (`chainSite` embedding, eventual-╬╡ form). `╬öEΓéÇ` and `╬ª` quantified outermost. | `IsAKLTChainDynamics` / `aklt_theorem_7_2` | **┬º7.1.3 Theorem 7.2** (Matsui; AXIOM, operator-algebraic): the AKLT model on the **infinite chain** has a **unique gapped ground state**. On the quasi-local C*-algebra, a state `╧ë` is a ground state iff `IsGroundState ╧ë ╬┤` (Def A.25: `╧ë(├éΓÇá[─ñ,├é])ΓëÑ0`) and has a nonzero gap iff `HasNonzeroGap ╧ë ╬┤ ╬│` (Def A.27, `╬│>0`). For the 1D `InfiniteSpinSystem` with the AKLT dynamics `╬┤` (tied by the uninterpreted marker `IsAKLTChainDynamics`), `aklt_theorem_7_2` gives `Γêâ ╧ë`, `IsState ╧ë`, `IsGroundState ╧ë ╬┤`, **unique among states** (`ΓêÇ╧ë', IsState ╧ë' ΓåÆ IsGroundState ╧ë' ╬┤ ΓåÆ ╧ë'=╧ë`), and `Γêâ╬│, HasNonzeroGap ╧ë ╬┤ ╬│`. The unique GS is the `LΓåæΓê₧` limit of the VBS state. Proof: Matsui (uniqueness) on AKLT; documented operator-algebra axiom (per the C*-algebra policy) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTInfiniteChain.lean` |

(7.1.4)): the AKLT gap is **stable** under small local perturbations. `perturbedAKLTHamiltonianS L ╬╡ v = akltHamiltonianS L + ╬╡ ╬ú_x v╠é_x` (eq. 7.1.4); `IsAKLTPerturbation L r vΓéÇ v` bundles: each `v╠é_x` Hermitian, `r`-local (`IsLocalRangeR`), bounded (`manyBodyOperatorNormS Γëñ vΓéÇ`), and translation-covariant (`IsTranslationCovariant`, an uninterpreted marker for `v╠é_x = T╠é^x v╠é_o T╠é^{ΓêÆx}`). For range `r` bounded by `vΓéÇ`, Γêâ `╬╡ΓéÇ>0` such that for `|╬╡|<╬╡ΓéÇ` there are `╬öE,C,╬╛>0` **L-independent** with: for any `L ΓëÑ 3` and any such `v`, the perturbed AKLT chain has a **unique ground state** (`IsUniqueChainGroundState`), a **gap ΓëÑ ╬öE** (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`), and **exponential decay of the connected correlation** `|connectedChainCorrelation| Γëñ C e^{ΓêÆd(x,y)/╬╛}` (`ringDist`). The **connected/truncated** correlation `Γƒ¿┼£_x┬╖┼£_yΓƒ⌐ ΓêÆ ╬ú_╬▒Γƒ¿┼£_x^(╬▒)Γƒ⌐Γƒ¿┼£_y^(╬▒)Γƒ⌐` is used since a symmetry-breaking perturbation can give nonzero one-point functions (the raw correlation need not decay); `L ΓëÑ 3` excludes the degenerate small rings. The gapful AKLT phase is stable. Proof: cluster expansion (Yarotsky) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTStability.lean` |

(7.1.5), (7.1.19)ΓÇô(7.1.20)): the bond projection onto total spin 2 and the singlet form. `bondSpin2ProjectionS x y = ┬╜(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y) + ΓàÖ(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)┬▓ + Γàô┬╖1` is the projection `P╠éΓéé[┼£_x+┼£_y]` onto the bond total-spin-2 subspace. `aklt_bond_term_eq_bondSpin2Projection` (**PROVED**, eq. 7.1.5) records the affine identity `┼£_x┬╖┼£_y + Γàô(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)┬▓ = 2 P╠éΓéé[┼£_x+┼£_y] ΓêÆ Γàö`, so the AKLT local term penalizes only the spin-2 component of each bond. `tasaki_lemma_7_4` (**AXIOM**, eqs. 7.1.19ΓÇô7.1.20): for `1 < L` (so the bond `{x, ringSucc x}` is genuinely two-site), a state `╬ª` is annihilated by the bond projection, `P╠éΓéé[┼£_x+┼£_{x+1}] ╬ª = 0`, **iff** `╬ª` has the valence-bond-solid singlet-tensor form on that bond (`IsVBSGroundForm`, an uninterpreted marker for the duplicated-`S=┬╜` / Schwinger-boson singlet construction not yet formalized). The AKLT ground space = states annihilated by every bond projection = the VBS state (KennedyΓÇôLiebΓÇôTasaki). (7.2.6)ΓÇô(7.2.8)): the AKLT VBS ground state has positive string order. `spinSStringPhaseS1 = diag(ΓêÆ1,1,ΓêÆ1)` is the single-site string phase `exp(i╧Ç┼£^z)` for `S=1` (entry `(ΓêÆ1)^{k+1}`). `stringOperatorS x y` is the diagonal string operator `exp(i╧Ç ╬ú_{x<z<y} ┼£_z^{(3)})` (phase `ΓêÅ_{x<z<y}(ΓêÆ1)^{╧â_z+1}`). `stringCorrelationS x y ╬ª = ΓêÆΓƒ¿╬ª\|┼£_x^{(3)} exp(i╧Ç╬ú┼£^{(3)}) ┼£_y^{(3)}\|╬ªΓƒ⌐` (= `S^{(3)}_{x,y}`, eq. 7.2.6, with the leading minus; the raw expectation is negative, ΓåÆ `ΓêÆ4/9` for VBS, so the minus makes it positive, and `O_string^{(3)} = lim lim S^{(3)}_{x,y}`). `aklt_string_order_7_2_8` (**AXIOM**, eq. 7.2.8): for the AKLT VBS ground-state family (`IsAKLTVBSGroundStateFamily`, an uninterpreted marker), the den NijsΓÇôRommelse string order parameter `O_string^{(3)}(╬ª_VBS) = lim_{dΓåæΓê₧} lim_{LΓåæΓê₧} stringCorrelationS = 4/9 > 0` (explicit double-╬╡ form over ordered linear windows `0 < x < y < L` with separation `d = yΓêÆx`, inner `LΓåæΓê₧` first), so the VBS state has hidden AFM order in all three directions (SU(2)) while N├⌐el order vanishes ΓÇö the hallmark of the Haldane phase. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L ├ù ZMod L) ├ù Fin 2` of an `L├ùL` torus (`2L┬▓` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `╧â^z`/`╧â^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = ΓêÆ╬ú_v ΓêÅ_{A_v} ╧â^z ΓêÆ ╬ú_p ΓêÅ_{B_p} ╧â^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap ΓëÑ 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L ╬╡ V = ─ñ_tc + ╬╡ ╬ú_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `╬╡ΓéÇ,╬ö>0` such that for `|╬╡|Γëñ╬╡ΓéÇ`, all `LΓëÑ2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `╬ö` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) ΓÇö topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |
