| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic lemma: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `╧â`, `Γƒ¿basisVec ╧â, ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y ┬╖ basisVec ╧âΓƒ⌐ = -1/4`. The 1-line foundation for every N├⌐el-bond expectation | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `inner_basisVec_szsz_basisVec` | generic `Γƒ¿basisVec ╧â, ┼£^(3)_x ┬╖ ┼£^(3)_y ┬╖ basisVec ╧âΓƒ⌐ = spinHalfSign ╧â x ┬╖ spinHalfSign ╧â y`. The diagonal-only spin-spin correlator on a basis state | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `neelCubicState_inner_spinHalfDot_{x,y,z}_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_{x,y,z}_wrap_` | 3D N├⌐el: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. | `neelChainState_energy_expectation_K1` | `K = 1` (2-site) open-chain Heisenberg energy expectation `Γƒ¿╬ª_N├⌐el, H_open ┬╖ ╬ª_N├⌐elΓƒ⌐ = J/2`. Combines `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` (`H = -2J ┬╖ spinHalfDot 0 1`) with the per-bond `-1/4` expectation, giving `-2J ┬╖ (-1/4) = J/2` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelConfigOf` / `neelStateOf` | generic graph-centric N├⌐el state from a sublattice indicator `A : V ΓåÆ Bool`: `neelConfigOf A x := if A x then Γåæ else Γåô` and `neelStateOf A := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`. The chain / 2D / 3D `neelXyzConfig` / `neelXyzState` definitions are bridged via `_eq_neelConfigOf` / `_eq_neelStateOf`. Tasaki ┬º2.5 eq. (2.5.2) graph-centric form | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` action on the canonical N├⌐el state: for any `x Γëá y` with `A x Γëá A y`, `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y ┬╖ ╬ª_N├⌐el(A) = (1/2) ┬╖ |swap_{x, y} ╬ª_N├⌐el(A)Γƒ⌐ - (1/4) ┬╖ ╬ª_N├⌐el(A)`. Tasaki ┬º2.5 eq. (2.5.3) graph-centric form. The chain / 2D / 3D `_adjacent` / `_wrap` bond actions are 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_spinHalfDot_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` expectation on the canonical N├⌐el state: for any `x Γëá y` with `A x Γëá A y`, `Γƒ¿╬ª_N├⌐el(A), ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y ┬╖ ╬ª_N├⌐el(A)Γƒ⌐ = -(1/4)`. Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼£^z_x ┬╖ ┼£^z_y` correlation on the canonical N├⌐el state: for any `A x Γëá A y`, `Γƒ¿╬ª_N├⌐el(A), ┼£^z_x ┬╖ ┼£^z_y ┬╖ ╬ª_N├⌐el(A)Γƒ⌐ = -(1/4)`. Diagonal half of Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.4) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `ΓêÅ_{x Γêê A} (-1)^(╧â x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V ΓåÆ Bool`, and configuration `╧â : V ΓåÆ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^Γåô)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `ΓêÅ_{x even} (-1)^(╧â x)`; specialisation to the N├⌐el configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬º2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `ΓêÅ_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(╧â (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) ├ù Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard N├⌐el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `ΓêÅ_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(╧â ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) ├ù Fin (2L)) ├ù Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard N├⌐el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig ╧â) = (-1)^K ┬╖ marshallSignChainConfig K ╧â`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)┬╖(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K ╧â ΓÇó basisVec ╧â`; specialisation at the N├⌐el configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the N├⌐el state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the N├⌐el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the N├⌐el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* N├⌐el configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) ΓÇö direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* N├⌐el state coincides with the time-reversed N├⌐el state in 1D, 2D, 3D ΓÇö both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. | `marshallDressedBasis A ╧â` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A ╧â ΓÇó basisVec ╧â` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V ΓåÆ Bool` (Tasaki ┬º2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `Γëñ 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the PerronΓÇôFrobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `╬¿╠â^╧â ╧â = marshallSignOf A ╧â`; `╬¿╠â^╧â ╧ä = 0` for `╧ä Γëá ╧â`; explicit `╬¿╠â^╧â ╧ä = marshallSignOf A ╧â ┬╖ basisVec ╧â ╧ä` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `┬▒1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A ╧â)┬▓ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `╬ú_╧ä ╬¿╠â^╧â ╧ä ┬╖ ╬¿╠â^╧ü ╧ä = if ╧ü = ╧â then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{╧â╠ä/2}` as the underlying `basisVec ╧â` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `╬ú_x ╧â_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) ╧â ╧â').im = 0` for all `x, y, ╧â, ╧â'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis theorem (Tasaki ┬º2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : ╬¢ ΓåÆ ╬¢ ΓåÆ Γäé` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬ú_{x,y} J(x,y) ┬╖ spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) ╧â ╧â').im = 0`. Γä¥-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A ╧â` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A ╧â).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `┬▒1 Γêê Γä¥` (either `1` or `(-1 : Γäé)^(╧â x : Γäò)` with `(╧â x : Γäò) Γêê {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `╬ú_╧ä \|╬¿╠â^╧âΓƒ⌐ ╧ä ┬╖ (H ┬╖ \|╬¿╠â^{╧â'}Γƒ⌐) ╧ä` is real (Tasaki ┬º2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). Reduces to `marshallSignOf A ╧â ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧â' ┬╖ H ╧â ╧â'` (each factor real) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `╬ú_╧ä \|╬¿╠â^╧âΓƒ⌐ ╧ä ┬╖ (M ┬╖ \|╬¿╠â^{╧â'}Γƒ⌐) ╧ä = marshallSignOf A ╧â ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧â' ┬╖ M ╧â ╧â'`. Generalises the inner-product computation used in Property (i) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x Γëá A y`) with `╧â` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`╧â x Γëá ╧â y`), `marshallSignOf A ╧â * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap ╧â x y) = -1`. The combined product over `╬¢` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(┬▒1)┬▓ = 1`; at the unique site in `A Γê⌐ {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(╧â x + ╧â y) = -1` since `╧â x Γëá ╧â y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `╧â Γëá ╧â'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A ╧â ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧â' ┬╖ J(x,y) ┬╖ (spinHalfDot x y) ╧â ╧â'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. Case analysis on `(spinHalfDot x y) ╧â ╧â'` (zero off-diagonal except at `╧â = basisSwap ╧â' x y`, antiparallel ╧â', `x Γëá y`) combined with the Marshall sign relation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ┬º2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `╧â Γëá ╧â'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `╬ú_╧ä \|╬¿╠â^╧âΓƒ⌐ ╧ä ┬╖ (H ┬╖ \|╬¿╠â^{╧â'}Γƒ⌐) ╧ä` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. | `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `╧â Γåª basisSwap ╧â x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `╧â x Γëá ╧â y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `╧â x Γëá ╧â y`, `SwapReachable G ╧â (basisSwap ╧â x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `╧â z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `╧â x Γëá ╧â y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 p. 41) ΓÇö combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronΓÇôFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `HΓéÇIndex ╬¢` | index type `{╧â : ╬¢ ΓåÆ Fin 2 // magnetization ╬¢ ╧â = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `HΓéÇIndex ╬¢` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A ╧â ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧ä ┬╖ (H_J)_{╧â,╧ä})` ΓÇö the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronΓÇôFrobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `╧â Γëá ╧ä`, packaged from PR ╬▒-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap ╧â x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap ╧â x y = ╧â Γêÿ Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `╬¢`); the magnetisation `Γêæ_z spinSign(╧â z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). Key ingredient for Tasaki ┬º2.5 p. 42 Proposition (equal-magnetisation reachability) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetization.lean` |
| `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `╧â` and `╧â'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `╧â Γëá ╧â'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `╧â x = 0, ╧â' x = 1`) and `y` (with `╧â y = 1, ╧â' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `╧â Γëá ╧â'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x Γêê D01`, `y Γêê D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `╧â'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `╧â'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬º2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `╧â`, `╧â'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `ΓëÑ 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronΓÇôFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c┬╖I ΓêÆ M` on `HΓéÇIndex ╬¢`. Used as input to PerronΓÇôFrobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c ΓëÑ M ╧â ╧â` for all `╧â`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) ╧â (basisSwap ╧â x y) = 1/2` for `x Γëá y` and antiparallel `╧â_x Γëá ╧â_y`. Building block for the explicit Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations needed for PerronΓÇôFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotSwapEntry.lean` |
| `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x Γëá y`, `╧â_x Γëá ╧â_y`, and `(u, v) Γêë {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap ╧â x y) u v Γëá ╧â`. Site analysis: `╧â` and `╧â' = basisSwap ╧â x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `╧â`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. Used for off-bond vanishing in the Heisenberg matrix entry computation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapEntry.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `╧â' = basisSwap ╧â x y` (with `x Γëá y`, `╧â_x Γëá ╧â_y`) and any `(u, v) Γêë {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) ╧â ╧â' = 0`. Three cases: `u = v` (diagonal), `u Γëá v` parallel ╧â' (constant action), `u Γëá v` antiparallel + off-bond (combinatorial helper) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotOffBond.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) ╧â (basisSwap ╧â x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. Decomposes the double sum and uses ╬▒-5e (active bond = 1/2) + ╬▒-5g (off-bond = 0). For symmetric `J`, simplifies to `J x y` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick ├ù Heisenberg formula ├ù symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. Combined with `J(x, y).re > 0` on graph edges gives strict negativity of M off-diagonal at swap pairs, hence strict positivity of `B = c┬╖I ΓêÆ M` ΓÇö the input for PerronΓÇôFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/DressedSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B ╧â ╧ä` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. Combines the dressed matrix value `-J(x, y).re` (PR ╬▒-5i) with the off-diagonal definition `B ╧â ╧ä = -M ╧â ╧ä` (PR ╬▒-5d). Single-step strict positivity for PerronΓÇôFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (╬▒-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) ╧â ╧ä > 0` and `B ╧ä ╧ü > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) ╧â ╧ü > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `╧â : HΓéÇIndex ╬¢` and any `╬╛` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) ╧â.val ╬╛`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) ╧â Γƒ¿╬╛, h_magΓƒ⌐ > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using ╬▒-5j (single-step swap positivity) and ╬▒-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). Key bridge from combinatorial reachability to PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ┬╖ I ΓêÆ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M ╧â ╧â` (strict). Cases on `╧â = ╧ä` (use diagonal positivity) vs `╧â Γëá ╧ä` (use ╬▒-5c reachability + ╬▒-5m matrix-power lift). Final input for PerronΓÇôFrobenius application | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedIrreducible.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR ╬▒-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronΓÇôFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |

| `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `╬¢` from sublattice indicator `A : ╬¢ ΓåÆ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x Γëá A y`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_eq_zero_iff_card_zero` | **iff characterisation of vanishing symm-predicted min energy** at `N ΓëÑ 1`: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N = 0 Γåö \|A\| = 0 Γê¿ \|┬¼A\| = 0`. Reverse direction (saturated-edge ΓåÆ energy 0) is PR #2849; forward direction (energy 0 ΓåÆ saturated edge) is the contrapositive of PR #2845's strict negativity (PR #2864, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmEqZeroIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_eq_zero_iff_card_zero` | **norm-zero characterisation** at `N ΓëÑ 1`: `ΓÇûbipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A NΓÇû = 0 Γåö \|A\| = 0 Γê¿ \|┬¼A\| = 0`. Direct from `norm_eq_zero` + PR #2864 (PR #2865, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNormEqZeroIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_pos_of_card_ne_zero` | **alternative form of symm-energy norm positivity**: `0 < ΓÇûbipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A NΓÇû` when `N ΓëÑ 1`, `\|A\| Γëá 0`, `\|┬¼A\| Γëá 0`. Contrapositive of PR #2865 (PR #2866, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNormPosOfCardNeZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_im_zero` / `_re_eq` | **`bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm` is real**: imaginary part is `0` and real part is `-\|A\|┬╖\|┬¼A\|┬╖N┬▓/2 - min(\|A\|, \|┬¼A\|)┬╖N` (with `min` taken in `Γä¥` after `Nat.cast_min`). Mirrors PR #2825 for the asymmetric form (PR #2843, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReal.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_nonpos` | **non-positivity of the predicted symmetric min energy**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re Γëñ 0`. Both terms `-\|A\|┬╖\|┬¼A\|┬╖N┬▓/2` and `-min(\|A\|, \|┬¼A\|)┬╖N` are non-positive. Symmetric-form mirror of PR #2791 (asymmetric `_re_nonpos`) (PR #2844, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNonpos.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_neg_of_nondegenerate` | **strict negativity of the predicted symmetric min energy** (non-degenerate case): `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re < 0` when both `\|A\| ΓëÑ 1`, `\|┬¼A\| ΓëÑ 1`, and `N ΓëÑ 1`. Symmetric-form mirror of PR #2817 (PR #2845, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmStrictNeg.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_eq` | **norm of the predicted symmetric min energy**: `ΓÇûbipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A NΓÇû = \|A\|┬╖\|┬¼A\|┬╖N┬▓/2 + min(\|A\|, \|┬¼A\|)┬╖N`. Combines PR #2843's `_re_eq` + `_im_zero` with PR #2844's `_re_nonpos`: norm = `|.re| = -.re` for a non-positive real value, then expand (PR #2848, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNorm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **symmetric predicted min energy vanishes at saturated edges**: at `\|┬¼A\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N = 0`. Both contributions vanish: cross term via the `0` factor and `min(\|A\|, \|┬¼A\|) = 0`. Symmetric-form mirror of PR #2779 (asymmetric saturated zero) (PR #2849, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmSaturated.lean`) |

Lifts the diagonal matrix element to the state expectation via `basisVecS_expectation_eq_diagonal` (spin-S) and `basisVec_expectation_eq_diagonal` (spin-1/2) (╬│-4 step 157) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1204) |
| `neelStateOfS_expectation_spinSDot_of_same` / `neelStateOf_expectation_spinHalfDot_of_same` | per-pair FM same-sublattice N├⌐el correlation: `<╬ª_N├⌐el \| ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y \| ╬ª_N├⌐el> = +N┬▓/4` (spin-S) and `+1/4` (spin-1/2) for any same-sublattice pair `x Γëá y` with `A x = A y`. Positive sign reflects parallel alignment within each sublattice. Companion to ╬│-4 step 157; relies on the parallel-diagonal lemmas `spinSDot_apply_diag_neelConfigOfS_of_same` and `spinHalfDot_apply_diag_neelConfigOf_of_same` (the latter using a new `spinHalfDot_apply_diag_of_ne_parallel`) (╬│-4 step 158) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1205) |
| `neelStateOfS_expectation_spinSDot_self` / `neelStateOf_expectation_spinHalfDot_self` | same-site (diagonal) per-pair N├⌐el correlation: `<╬ª_N├⌐el \| ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_x \| ╬ª_N├⌐el> = N(N+2)/4` (spin-S) and `3/4` (spin-1/2). Universal local Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)`, completing the cross/same/self trio of per-pair correlations. | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├⌐el**: `<╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├⌐el(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)┬▓+\|╬¢\|/2`) + ╬│-4 step 232 (`M(┬¼A) = ΓêÆM(A)`): `(M)┬▓` is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├⌐el**: `<╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├⌐el(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS┬▓` is sign-flip invariant; `╬ú_x m_x┬▓` evaluates to `\|V\|┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for both A and ┬¼A. Spin-`S` mirror of ╬│-4 step 233 (╬│-4 step 234) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1281) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├⌐el**: `<╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├⌐el(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├⌐el**: `<╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├⌐el(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├⌐el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 235 (╬│-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£^1)┬▓+(┼£^2)┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├⌐el**: equal expectations on `╬ª_N├⌐el(A)` and `╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|┬╖N/2` via ╬│-4 step 220 + 221 (╬│-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N├⌐el**: both expectations equal `\|╬¢\|/2`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 219 (╧â-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 237 (╬│-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├⌐el**: `<╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬ª_N├⌐el(┬¼A)> = ΓêÆ<╬ª_N├⌐el(A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬ª_N├⌐el(A)>`. | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├⌐el**. Direct from ╬│-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 239 (╬│-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N├⌐el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<╬ª_N├⌐el\|┼£_tot^(1,2)\|╬ª_N├⌐el> = 0`. Trivial corollary of ╬│-4 step 214 applied to `╧â = neelConfigOfS A N` (╬│-4 step 241). Together with ╬│-4 step 91 (`<┼£_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|ΓêÆ\|┬¼A\|)┬╖N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N├⌐el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ╬│-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ╬│-4 step 215 (╬│-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ╬ú_{j=1}^z ┼£_0 ┬╖ ┼£_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `ΓêÆS(1 + zS)` (╬│-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j Γêê univ.erase 0` (╬│-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = Γêà` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `ΓêÆS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z ΓëÑ 1` (╬│-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ª_Γèñ\|H\|╬ª_Γèñ> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `ΓêÆS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (╬│-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<╬ª_aligned(c)\|H\|╬ª_aligned(c)> = z┬╖(N/2 ΓêÆ c.val)┬▓`. Generalises ╬│-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` (╬│-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<╬ª_ΓèÑ\|H\|╬ª_ΓèÑ> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓`. Direct specialisation of ╬│-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (╬│-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `┼£_R^(╬▒) = ╬ú_{j Γëá 0} onSiteS j ┼£^(╬▒)` for axes `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `┼£^(╬▒)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (┼£_tot┬▓ ΓêÆ ┼£_0┬▓ ΓêÆ ┼£_R┬▓)/2` (╬│-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`┼£_0 ┬╖ ┼£_R` decomposition**: `H = ╬ú_╬▒ onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) ┬╖ leafSpinSOp_╬▒`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `╬ú_j (A * B_j) = A * (╬ú_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (╬│-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_╬▒ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) + leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (╬│-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `┼£_R┬▓ := (┼£_R^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_R^(2))┬▓ + (┼£_R^(3))┬▓`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (╬│-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 ┼£^(╬▒)) (leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (╬│-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2┬╖H = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ΓêÆ ┼£_0┬▓ ΓêÆ ┼£_R┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `┼£_0┬▓ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `┼£_R┬▓ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `╬ú_╬▒ totalSpinSOp_╬▒┬▓ = ╬ú_╬▒ (onSite 0 + leaf_╬▒)┬▓` via ╬│-5 step 251 + `(a+b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` (using ╬│-5 step 253 commutativity) + ╬│-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (╬│-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Γäé-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : Γäé) ΓÇó H = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ΓêÆ ┼£_0┬▓ ΓêÆ ┼£_R┬▓`. Direct corollary of ╬│-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (╬│-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 ┬╖ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot┬▓\|v> ΓêÆ <v\|S0┬▓\|v> ΓêÆ <v\|SR┬▓\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of ╬│-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (╬│-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0┬▓` term in the Casimir decomposition (╬│-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 ┬╖ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot┬▓\|v> ΓêÆ (N(N+2)/4)┬╖<v\|v> ΓêÆ <v\|SR┬▓\|v>`. Combines ╬│-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0┬▓` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (╬│-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `Stot┬▓┬╖v = ╬▒┬╖v` and `SR┬▓┬╖v = ╬▓┬╖v`, then `H┬╖v = (╬▒ ΓêÆ N(N+2)/4 ΓêÆ ╬▓)/2 ┬╖ v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(╬▒, ╬▓)` joint eigenvalues (╬│-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N ┬╖ v = (N(N+2)/4) ΓÇó v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from ╬│-5 step 259 (╬│-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_╬▒ 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = Γêà` in `Fin 1` (╬│-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = ╬ú_{j,k Γêê univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (╬│-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ª_Γèñ\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|╬ª_Γèñ> = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = z┬╖(N/2)` is max leaf spin. Computed by rearranging ╬│-5 step 254 (Casimir decomposition) and applying closed forms for `<Stot┬▓>`, `<S0┬▓>`, `<H>` (╬│-5 step 263) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1310) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form on allUp**: `H ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐ = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐`. Proof: each leaf-pair `spinSDot 0 j` at `j Γëá 0` gives `(N┬▓/4)┬╖\|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐` (via `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne`); sum over `z` leaves (╬│-5 step 264) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1311) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form** of `┼£_R┬▓` on allUp: `┼£_R┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐ = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐`. Witnesses that `\|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐` is in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`. Combined with ╬│-5 step 264 + existing `Stot┬▓` eigenvector identity, confirms `\|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐` is a joint eigenstate of `H, Stot┬▓, ┼£_0┬▓, ┼£_R┬▓`. Proof: rearrange ╬│-5 step 255 + apply (╬│-5 step 265) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1312) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form on allDown**: `H ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐ = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐`. AllDown mirror of ╬│-5 step 264; same eigenvalue (spin-flip symmetry) (╬│-5 step 266) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1313) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form** of `┼£_R┬▓` on allDown: `┼£_R┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐ = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ \|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐`. AllDown mirror of ╬│-5 step 265; both `\|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐` and `\|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐` lie in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`, differing only by total `┼£_tot^(3)` magnetization (╬│-5 step 267) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1314) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_gs_casimir_sector` | **GS-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `┼£_tot┬▓` at `((zΓêÆ1)N/2)((zΓêÆ1)N/2+1)` and `┼£_R┬▓` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ┬╖ v = ΓêÆ(N/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ v = ΓêÆS(1+zS) ┬╖ v`. Specialization of ╬│-5 step 259 to the ground-state sector predicted by Tasaki Problem 2.5.a (╬│-5 step 268) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1315) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_max_casimir_sector` | **Max-Casimir-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `┼£_tot┬▓` at `((z+1)N/2)((z+1)N/2+1)` and `┼£_R┬▓` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H ┬╖ v = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ ┬╖ v = zS┬▓ ┬╖ v`. Maximum Casimir sector containing `\|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐, \|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐` (╬│-5 step 269) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1316) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_gs_energy_smul` | **Predicted ground-state energy** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a target): `singleClusterGSEnergyS z N := ΓêÆ(N/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) = ΓêÆS(1+zS)`, with named eigenvalue identity `H ┬╖ v = singleClusterGSEnergyS z N ΓÇó v` at the GS Casimir sector (╬│-5 step 270) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1317) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_max_energy_smul` | **Maximum Casimir-sector energy**: `singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N := z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ = zS┬▓`, with named eigenvalue identity `H ┬╖ v = singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N ΓÇó v` at the max Casimir sector containing `\|╬ª_ΓèñΓƒ⌐, \|╬ª_ΓèÑΓƒ⌐` (╬│-5 step 271) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1318) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_nonneg` | **Energy real-part sign hygiene**: `Re(GS) Γëñ 0` (AFM physical bound) and `0 Γëñ Re(Max)` (extremal aligned-state eigenvalue is non-negative) (╬│-5 step 272) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1319) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re` | **Energy ordering**: `Re(GS) Γëñ Re(Max)`. Consistency check between ╬│-5 steps 268 and 269 (╬│-5 step 273) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1320) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_im_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_im_zero` | **Energies are real**: imaginary parts of both named single-cluster energies vanish (╬│-5 step 274) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1321) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_one_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_one_eq` | **Dimer (z=1) closed forms**: `singleClusterGSEnergyS 1 N = ΓêÆ(N/2)(N/2+1) = ΓêÆS(S+1)` (canonical singlet eigenvalue of `┼£_0┬╖┼£_1`); `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 1 N = (N/2)┬▓ = S┬▓` (canonical triplet eigenvalue) (╬│-5 step 275) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1322) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_left` | **Trivial edge cases**: `GS z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max 0 N = 0` (single-site, no leaves) (╬│-5 step 276) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1323) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_eq` | **Explicit `.re` forms** as `Γä¥` values: `Re(GS) = -(N/2)(zN/2+1)`, `Re(Max) = z┬╖N┬▓/4`. Useful for downstream real comparisons (╬│-5 step 278) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1325) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_sub_singleClusterGSEnergyS` | **GS-Max energy gap**: `Max ΓêÆ GS = (N/2)(zN+1) = S(2zS+1)`. Closed-form difference between the two named eigenvalues (╬│-5 step 280) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1327) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_lt_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_of_pos` | **Strict gap for `N ΓëÑ 1`**: `Re(GS) < Re(Max)` whenever spin is non-trivial (╬│-5 step 281) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1328) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_neg_of_pos` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_pos_of_pos` | **Strict sign**: `Re(GS) < 0` for `N ΓëÑ 1`; `0 < Re(Max)` for `z ΓëÑ 1, N ΓëÑ 1`. Strengthens ╬│-5 step 272 (╬│-5 step 283) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1330) |

| `exists_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under an endomorphism contains a non-zero eigenvector of that endomorphism. The proof restricts the endomorphism to the submodule and applies Mathlib's finite-dimensional eigenvalue-existence theorem. This is the linear-algebra bridge for the next Problem 2.5.a joint total/leaf Casimir sector-decomposition step after magnetization extraction (╬│-5 step 332) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4041) |
| `exists_joint_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Joint invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under two commuting endomorphisms contains a non-zero simultaneous eigenvector for both. The proof first extracts an eigenvector for the first endomorphism, then applies the one-operator bridge to the non-zero intersection of the original submodule with that eigenspace, using commutativity to prove invariance under the second endomorphism. This packages the next linear-algebra step for extracting total/leaf Casimir sector data inside the single-cluster magnetization/Hamiltonian component (╬│-5 step 333) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4042) |
| `singleCluster_totalSpinSSquared_commute_leafSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_commute_leafSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule` / `singleCluster_totalSpinSSquared_mulVecLin_commute_leafSpinSSquared_mulVecLin` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule_totalSpinSSquared_invariant` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule_leafSpinSSquared_invariant` / `exists_joint_casimir_eigenvector_in_singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule` | **Single-cluster Casimir invariance bridge**: the Casimir decomposition `(2 : Γäé) ΓÇó H = (┼£_tot)┬▓ - ┼£_0┬▓ - ┼£_R┬▓`, the scalar central-site Casimir, and the total/leaf sublattice-Casimir commutation theorem show that the single-cluster Hamiltonian commutes with both the total and leaf Casimirs. Consequently the Hamiltonian eigenspace intersected with a fixed magnetization subspace is invariant under both Casimirs, and any non-zero such submodule contains a non-zero joint total/leaf Casimir eigenvector when the caller supplies the explicit algebraically closed field hypothesis `[IsAlgClosed Γäé]` (╬│-5 step 334) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterCasimirInvariance.lean` (PR #4043) |
| `singleCluster_global_eigenvalue_lower_of_exists_joint_casimir_energy_lower` / `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_of_exists_joint_casimir_energy_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_hermitianMinEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_gs_sector_and_exists_joint_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_exists_gs_sector_and_exists_joint_lower` | **Same-energy joint-Casimir lower bridge**: the lower-bound callback no longer needs the original non-zero real-energy `H`-eigenvector to be a joint total/leaf Casimir eigenvector. | `singleCluster_exists_joint_casimir_energy_lower_of_mag_component_joint_lower` / `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_of_mag_component_joint_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_gs_sector_and_mag_joint_lower` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_eq_gs_of_exists_gs_sector_and_mag_joint_lower` | **Single-cluster joint-Casimir witness bridge**: magnetization extraction turns any non-zero real-energy Hamiltonian eigenvector into a non-zero same-energy magnetization component. A remaining Casimir-energy lower callback on such extracted joint vectors therefore yields the same-energy witness callback from PR #4044, plus Hermitian-min lower and conditional equality wrappers under the explicit `[IsAlgClosed Γäé]` hypothesis (╬│-5 step 336) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterJointCasimirWitness.lean` (PR #4045) |

Feeding this witness into the extracted coupled lower bridge identifies `hermitianMinEigenvalue H = Re (singleClusterGSEnergyS z N)` under `[IsAlgClosed Γäé]` (╬│-5 step 338) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterGSJointWitness.lean` (PR #4047) |
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` / `rayleighOnVec_sum_matrix` / `sum_lower_bounds_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_sum` / `add_lower_bounds_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue_add` / `tasaki25b_local_cluster_sum_lower_bound` / `tasaki25b_local_cluster_sum_lower_bound_closed_form` | **Problem 2.5.b sum lower-bound bridge**: packages Tasaki's Lemma A.5 argument for finite-dimensional Hermitian matrices. If each local Hamiltonian has a lower bound `╬╡ x`, then the Hermitian minimum eigenvalue of the sum is at least `╬ú x, ╬╡ x`. The local-cluster wrappers specialize this to the Problem 2.5.a star-cluster energies, yielding the abstract lower bound `E_GS ΓëÑ -╬ú_{xΓêêA} S(1 + degree(x) S)` with `S = N/2` once a graph Hamiltonian is decomposed into centered local cluster terms (╬│-6 step 339). The generic "a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian" helper has been extracted as `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` into `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` (PR #4341), replacing the per-file private copies in `Quantum/IsingChain.lean`, `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean`, and `Quantum/SpinS/TotalSpin.lean` | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianMinEigenvalueSumLower.lean` (PR #4048), `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` (PR #4341) |
| `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` / `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_one_eq_sum_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS` / `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_eq_sum_filter_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS` | **Problem 2.5.b graph-local decomposition**: defines the same-Hilbert-space local star Hamiltonian `h_x = ╬ú_{yΓêêN_G(x)} ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y`. The unit-coupling graph Hamiltonian is `╬ú_x h_x` under the repository's ordered-pair convention, and on a bipartite graph the half-coupling Hamiltonian is the one-sided sum `╬ú_{xΓêêA} h_x`, using the pair swap and `spinSDot_comm`. | `graphLocalOutsideSite` / `graphLocalOutsideSite_fintype` / `graphLocalOutsideSite_decidableEq` / `graphLocalOutsideConfigExtend` / `graphLocalProductConfig` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_none` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_some` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_outside` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_symm_apply` / `graphLocalProductConfig_outside` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_isHermitian` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_apply_of_outside_eq` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_apply_of_outside_ne` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_mulVec` / `matrix_mulVec_reindex_comp_symm` / `dotProduct_comp_equiv_symm` / `rayleighOnVec_reindex_comp_symm` / `dotProduct_product_re_eq_sum_blocks` / `rayleighOnVec_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_rayleigh_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_minEigenvalue_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_rayleigh_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_lower` | **Problem 2.5.b graph-local product lower-bound bridge**: reindexes a full graph configuration around `x` as an option-star configuration paired with an outside assignment. In these product coordinates the graph-local star is block diagonal in the outside coordinate, each diagonal block is the option-star Hamiltonian, and the Rayleigh numerator and squared norm decompose as sums over outside blocks. Reindex-invariance lemmas transfer the result back to the original same-Hilbert-space graph-local star. | `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_cross_commute` | the two sublattice Casimirs commute: `Commute (┼£_A)┬▓ (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`. | `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | **Sublattice SU(2) algebra**: `[┼£_A^(╬▒), ┼£_A^(╬▓)] = i ╬╡^╬▒╬▓╬│ ┼£_A^(╬│)` for each cyclic axis triple. Generic helper `sublatticeSpin_commutator_general` lifts the single-site commutator `[S╬▒, S╬▓] = i ΓÇó S╬│` to the sublattice sum (off-diagonal pairs vanish by `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne`; diagonal contributes `if A x then i ΓÇó onSite x S╬│ else 0`) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | **Sublattice Casimir self-invariance**: `Commute (┼£_A)┬▓ (┼£_A^(╬▒))` for each axis. Standard SU(2) Casimir argument applied at the sublattice level: per-axis Leibniz rule `[X┬▓, C] = X[X,C] + [X,C]X` combined with the sublattice SU(2) algebra. Together with cross-commute, gives `Commute (┼£_A)┬▓ (┼£_tot^(╬▒))`, hence `Commute (┼£_A)┬▓ (┼£_tot)┬▓` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_complement` / `_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `(┼£_A)┬▓` commutes with each `┼£_┬¼A^(╬▒)` (`Commute.mul_left` over the mixed-axes cross-commute), with each `┼£_tot^(╬▒) = ┼£_A^(╬▒) + ┼£_┬¼A^(╬▒)`, and with `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = ╬ú_╬▒ (┼£_tot^(╬▒))┬▓`. Provides the **third pairwise commutativity** needed for the joint eigenbasis of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` (the first two are ╬▒-6r self-invariance and ╬▒-6o inter-Casimir cross-commute) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ┬º2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y = (1/2)(┼£_x^+ ┼£_y^- + ┼£_x^- ┼£_y^+) + ┼£_x^(3) ┼£_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
