| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` | `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = ╬ú_{x,y} ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfPairSpinSq` / `spinHalfPairSpinSq_eq` | `(┼£_x + ┼£_y)┬▓ = 2┬╖(┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y) + ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_x + ┼£_y ┬╖ ┼£_y` (Tasaki (2.2.18)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y, ┼£_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` for ╬▒ Γêê {1, 2, 3} (SU(2) invariance, Tasaki (2.2.17)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y, ┼£^┬▒_tot] = 0` (ladder-operator version of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_parallel` | `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y |╧âΓƒ⌐ = (1/4) |╧âΓƒ⌐` when `╧â x = ╧â y` and `x Γëá y` (Tasaki (2.2.19) parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y |ΓåæΓåæΓƒ⌐ = (1/4) |ΓåæΓåæΓƒ⌐`, `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y |ΓåôΓåôΓƒ⌐ = (1/4) |ΓåôΓåôΓƒ⌐` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = ┬▒1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `basisSwap` / `basisSwap_involutive` / `basisSwap_antiparallel` | site-swap of `╧â` at `x, y`, involutive and preserves anti-parallelism | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_antiparallel` | `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y |╧âΓƒ⌐ = (1/2) |swap ╧âΓƒ⌐ - (1/4) |╧âΓƒ⌐` when `╧â x Γëá ╧â y` (anti-parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_singlet` | singlet eigenvalue `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y (|╧âΓƒ⌐ - |swap ╧âΓƒ⌐) = -(3/4) (|╧âΓƒ⌐ - |swap ╧âΓƒ⌐)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) singlet `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_triplet_anti` | triplet `m = 0` eigenvalue `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y (|╧âΓƒ⌐ + |swap ╧âΓƒ⌐) = (1/4) (|╧âΓƒ⌐ + |swap ╧âΓƒ⌐)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian` | `H = ╬ú_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` (general Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[H, ┼£_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` for all axes (Tasaki (2.2.13) SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, ┼£^┬▒_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H ┼£tot┬▓` ΓÇö the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, ┼£tot^╬▒] = 0` for each ╬▒, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `┼£tot┬▓ ┬╖ v = S ┬╖ v` then `┼£tot┬▓ ┬╖ (H ┬╖ v) = S ┬╖ (H ┬╖ v)` ΓÇö operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `╧â^+ ┬╖ ╧â^z = -╧â^+` and `╧â^z ┬╖ ╧â^+ = ╧â^+` ΓÇö the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `┼£_tot┬▓ |s s ΓÇª sΓƒ⌐ = (N(N+2)/4) |s s ΓÇª sΓƒ⌐` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : Γäò`, `┼£tot┬▓ ┬╖ (┼£tot^Γêô)^k ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐ = (|╬¢|┬╖(|╬¢|+2)/4) ┬╖ (┼£tot^Γêô)^k ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐` ΓÇö the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |╬¢|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki ┬º2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐ = (╬ú_{x,y} J(x,y)┬╖(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐` ΓÇö every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ┬º2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-┼£_x┬╖┼£_y |╬ªΓåæΓƒ⌐ = -S┬▓ |╬ªΓåæΓƒ⌐` for `S = 1/2`, `x Γëá y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) ΓÇö the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|┬╖S┬▓`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H ┬╖ (┼£_tot^+ ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐) = c_J ┬╖ (┼£_tot^+ ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐)` ΓÇö `┼£_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `┼£_tot^-` ΓÇö the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|╬ª_MΓƒ⌐` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : Γäò`, `H ┬╖ ((┼£_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐) = c_J ┬╖ ((┼£_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 ΓÇö every iterate `(┼£_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |╬ªΓåæΓƒ⌐` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|╬ªΓåæΓƒ⌐` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `┼£_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : Γäò`, `H ┬╖ ((┼£_tot^+)^k ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐) = c_J ┬╖ ((┼£_tot^+)^k ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `╬╕ : Γä¥`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `├¢^(╬▒)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼£_tot^╬▒)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{╬▒}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{╬▒}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `╬╕`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ┬╖ (├¢^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐) = c_J ┬╖ (├¢^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐)` ΓÇö the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `╬╕`, `╧ò`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ┬╖ (├¢^(3)_╧ò ┬╖ ├¢^(2)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐) = c_J ┬╖ (├¢^(3)_╧ò ┬╖ ├¢^(2)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sΓÇªsΓƒ⌐)` ΓÇö the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|╬₧_╬╕,╧òΓƒ⌐` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `┼£_tot┬▓ (|ΓåæΓåôΓƒ⌐ - |ΓåôΓåæΓƒ⌐) = 0` for `╬¢ = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `┼£_tot┬▓ (|ΓåæΓåôΓƒ⌐ + |ΓåôΓåæΓƒ⌐) = 2(|ΓåæΓåôΓƒ⌐ + |ΓåôΓåæΓƒ⌐)` for `╬¢ = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `┼£_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x S╬▒, ╬ú_z onSite z S╬▓] = onSite x [S╬▒, S╬▓]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `╧â = 2┬╖S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ╬ú ╧â^z_i ╧â^z_{i+1} - h ╬ú ╧â^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `╧â^z_x ┬╖ ╧â^z_y` (generic in `╬¢`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : ╬¢ ΓåÆ ╬¢ ΓåÆ Γäé) (h : Γäé)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `╬ú J(x,y) ╧â^z_x ╧â^z_y ΓêÆ h ╬ú ╧â^x_x` for any finite `╬¢` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ╬▓ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `╬ú_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `╬ú_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i Γåª i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N├⌐el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬º2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 Γëí 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Testing infrastructure

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState ╬▓ J h N` | `gibbsState ╬▓ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `╧ü_╬▓` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[╧ü_╬▓, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `Γƒ¿[H_Ising, A]Γƒ⌐_╬▓ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(Γƒ¿H_IsingΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(Γƒ¿H_Ising ┬╖ OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(Γƒ¿H_Ising^2Γƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(Γƒ¿H_Ising^nΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` for any `n : Γäò` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Γƒ¿A┬╖B + B┬╖AΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Γƒ¿A┬╖B ΓêÆ B┬╖AΓƒ⌐_╬▓).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_╬▓(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ╬▓ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).re : Γäé) = Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(╧ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `Γƒ¿H_IsingΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = -J ┬╖ Γêæ Γƒ¿╧â^z ╧â^zΓƒ⌐_╬▓ + (-h) ┬╖ Γêæ Γƒ¿╧â^xΓƒ⌐_╬▓` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ┬º3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ╬▓ H` | `exp(-╬▓H) := Matrix.exp (-╬▓ ΓÇó H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0┬╖H) = 1` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, pp. 75ΓÇô78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(╬▓Γéü+╬▓Γéé)H) = exp(-╬▓ΓéüH) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓ΓééH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-╬▓(HΓéü+HΓéé)) = exp(-╬▓HΓéü) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓HΓéé)` for commuting `HΓéü, HΓéé` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-╬▓H) Γëá 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `╧ü_╬▓ Γëá 0` when `Z(╬▓) Γëá 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(╧ü_╬▓)Γü╗┬╣ = Z(╬▓) ┬╖ e^{╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) Γëá 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(╬▓) ┬╖ ╧ü_╬▓ = e^{-╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) Γëá 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(╬▓) ┬╖ Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = Tr(e^{-╬▓H} ┬╖ A)` when `Z(╬▓) Γëá 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬╖╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : Γäò` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2╬▓)H) = exp(-╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-╬▓H))Γü╗┬╣ = exp(╬▓H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬╖╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : Γäñ` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn ╬▓ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-╬▓H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (╬¢ ΓåÆ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) Γëá 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n Γäé`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn ╬▓ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(╧ü_╬▓┬▓) = Z(2╬▓) / Z(╬▓)┬▓` (purity / R├⌐nyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(╧ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for any `n : Γäò` (R├⌐nyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `╧ü_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `╧ü_0Γü╗┬╣ = dim ┬╖ I` (matrix inverse at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ╬▓ H` | `╧ü := (1/Z) ΓÇó exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(╧ü) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `╧ü` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation ╬▓ H O` | `Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐ := Matrix.trace (╧ü * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `Γƒ¿1Γƒ⌐ = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `Γƒ¿OΓéü + OΓééΓƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿OΓéüΓƒ⌐ + Γƒ¿OΓééΓƒ⌐` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `Γƒ¿c ┬╖ OΓƒ⌐ = c ┬╖ Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐` (scalar linearity, `c : Γäé`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `Γƒ¿-OΓƒ⌐ = -Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `Γƒ¿A - BΓƒ⌐ = Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐ - Γƒ¿BΓƒ⌐` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `Γƒ¿Γêæ i Γêê s, f iΓƒ⌐ = Γêæ i Γêê s, Γƒ¿f iΓƒ⌐` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-╬▓H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[╧ü_╬▓, H] = 0`, i.e. `╧ü_╬▓` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ┬╖ B)) = Tr(A ┬╖ B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n Γäé` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).re : Γäé) = Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `Γƒ¿H ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = Γƒ¿A ┬╖ HΓƒ⌐_╬▓` for any `A` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `Γƒ¿A ┬╖ OΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = Γƒ¿O ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `Γƒ¿A ┬╖ O ΓêÆ O ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `Γƒ¿[H, A]Γƒ⌐_╬▓ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(Γƒ¿HΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Γƒ¿O ┬╖ OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Γƒ¿O^nΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : Γäò` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance ╬▓ H O` | `Var_╬▓(O) := Γƒ¿O ┬╖ OΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ΓêÆ Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓┬▓` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_╬▓(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at ╬▓ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr(O┬▓) ΓêÆ ((1/dim) ┬╖ Tr O)┬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_╬▓(O) = Γƒ¿(O ΓêÆ Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ┬╖ 1) ┬╖ (O ΓêÆ Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ┬╖ 1)Γƒ⌐_╬▓` (centered-square form, `Z Γëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) := Γƒ¿A ┬╖ BΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ΓêÆ Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ┬╖ Γƒ¿BΓƒ⌐_╬▓` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) ΓêÆ Cov_╬▓(B, A) = Γƒ¿A ┬╖ B ΓêÆ B ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_╬▓(AΓéü + AΓéé, B) = Cov_╬▓(AΓéü, B) + Cov_╬▓(AΓéé, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, BΓéü + BΓéé) = Cov_╬▓(A, BΓéü) + Cov_╬▓(A, BΓéé)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_╬▓(c ΓÇó A, B) = c ┬╖ Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, c ΓÇó B) = c ┬╖ Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_╬▓(c ΓÇó 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_╬▓(A, c ΓÇó 1) = 0` (when `Z Γëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_╬▓(c ΓÇó 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_╬▓(A, c ΓÇó 1) = 0` (when `Z Γëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) := (1/2) ┬╖ Γƒ¿A ┬╖ B + B ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ΓêÆ Γƒ¿AΓƒ⌐_╬▓ ┬╖ Γƒ¿BΓƒ⌐_╬▓` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_╬▓` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_╬▓(A + B) = Var_╬▓(A) + Var_╬▓(B) + 2 ┬╖ Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_╬▓(1) = 0` (when `Z Γëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_╬▓(c ΓÇó A) = c┬▓ ┬╖ Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(c ΓÇó 1) = 0` (when `Z Γëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_╬▓(ΓêÆA) = Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(A + c ΓÇó 1) = Var_╬▓(A)` (when `Z Γëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) + (1/2) ┬╖ Γƒ¿A ┬╖ B ΓêÆ B ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = (1/2) ┬╖ (Cov_╬▓(A, B) + Cov_╬▓(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(╧ü ┬╖ X) = Tr(╧ü ┬╖ Xß┤┤)` for Hermitian `╧ü` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Γƒ¿A ┬╖ BΓƒ⌐_╬▓ = Γƒ¿B ┬╖ AΓƒ⌐_╬▓` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | `(Γƒ¿A┬╖B + B┬╖AΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` (anticommutator is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | `(Γƒ¿A┬╖B ΓêÆ B┬╖AΓƒ⌐_╬▓).re = 0` (commutator is purely imaginary) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | `(Γƒ¿H ┬╖ OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ┬º3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `╬¢ ΓåÆ ╬¢ ΓåÆ Γäé` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val Γê¿ ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) ΓåÆ Fin (N+1) ΓåÆ Γäé`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) ΓåÆ Fin (N+2) ΓåÆ Γäé`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬ú_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : Γäé`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) ╬▓ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: ╬▓ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `Γƒ¿[H, A]Γƒ⌐ = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓).re : Γäé) = Γƒ¿OΓƒ⌐_╬▓`, R├⌐nyi-n trace `Tr(╧ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph N ╬▓ G J U` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Hubbard Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardChainGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridge: `hubbardChainGibbsState = hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J) U` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract i` | Jordan-Wigner string for any `[Fintype ╬¢] [LinearOrder ╬¢]` ΓÇö product of `╧â^z_j` over `j < i`; generalises the Fin-specific `jwString` | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract_isHermitian` / `jwStringAbstract_sq` / `jwStringAbstract_commute_onSite` | basic structural identities | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract i` / `fermionCreationAbstract i` / `fermionNumberAbstract i` | abstract-╬¢ annihilation / creation / number; rfl-bridges to the Fin-specific versions | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionCreationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionNumberAbstract_isHermitian` | adjoint relations and Hermiticity in the abstract form | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_sq` / `fermionCreationAbstract_sq` | `c_i┬▓ = 0`, `c_iΓÇá┬▓ = 0` (Pauli exclusion) | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionMultiAnticommAbstract_self` / `fermionNumberAbstract_sq` | `{c_i, c_iΓÇá} = 1` and `n_i┬▓ = n_i` (abstract same-site CAR + idempotency) | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.boxProd_decidableAdj` | `DecidableRel (G Γûí H).Adj` for the box product (mathlib provides only the bare definition); enables 2D / nD lattices via `couplingOf` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph` | the **infinite** one-dimensional chain `Γäñ` as a `SimpleGraph` (= `hasse Γäñ`), the infinite-volume analogue of `pathGraph (N+1)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: `a ~ b Γåö b = a + 1 Γê¿ a = b + 1` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph` | the **infinite** 2D square lattice on `Γäñ ├ù Γäñ` as `integerChainGraph Γûí integerChainGraph`; infinite analogue of `squareLatticeCoupling` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerSquareLatticeGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: nearest neighbour in one coordinate, equal in the other | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicLatticeGraph` / `hypercubicLatticeGraph_adj` | the **infinite** `d`-dimensional hypercubic lattice `Γäñß╡ê` on `Fin d ΓåÆ Γäñ` as a `SimpleGraph` (adjacency = differ in exactly one coordinate by `┬▒1`, the nearest-neighbor bond set `BΓê₧`, Tasaki eq. (4.3.1)) + a `DecidableRel` instance; the substrate of the thermodynamic-limit / infinite-volume frontier (Issue #4557) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox` / `mem_hypercubicBox` | the centered finite box `╬¢_n = {x Γêê Γäñß╡ê : ΓêÆn < xß╡ó Γëñ n}` (even side `2n`, Tasaki eq. (3.1.2)) as a `Finset`, with its coordinatewise membership criterion | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox_subset_succ` / `hypercubicBox_monotone` / `exists_mem_hypercubicBox` / `iUnion_hypercubicBox` | the **monotone exhaustion** of `Γäñß╡ê` by the boxes: nesting `╬¢_n Γèå ╬¢_{n+1}`, monotonicity, every site lies in some box, and `ΓïâΓéÖ ╬¢_n = Γäñß╡ê`; the increasing-region API for constructing the thermodynamic limit | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox_card` | the box **volume** `\|╬¢_n\| = (2n)ß╡ê` (side `2n` in each of `d` directions); the volume normalization `Lß╡ê = (2n)ß╡ê` of the bulk-density / energy-density limits (Tasaki eqs. (4.3.4), (4.3.6)) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBoxGraph` / `hypercubicBoxVertex` / `hypercubicBoxGraph_adj` | the **finite-volume graph** on the box `╬¢_n` (a `Fintype` subtype of `Γäñß╡ê`): the induced subgraph `(hypercubicLatticeGraph d).induce Γåæ╬¢_n`, the graph-centric finite-volume substrate `╬¢_n Γå¬ Γäñß╡ê` of the thermodynamic limit | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBoxCoupling` / `hypercubicBoxParityColoring` / `hypercubicBoxGraph_isBipartite` | the uniform nearest-neighbor coupling `couplingOf` on `╬¢_n` (finite-volume many-body Hamiltonian input), and the inherited bipartite structure (parity coloring restricted from `Γäñß╡ê`) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `boxHeisenbergHamiltonianS` / `boxAFMHeisenbergHamiltonianS` (+ `_isHermitian`, `_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS_boxCoupling`) | the **concrete finite-volume spin-`S` Heisenberg model** on the box `╬¢_n Γèé Γäñß╡ê` (`ManyBodyOpS (hypercubicBoxVertex d n) N`), via the graph Hamiltonian on `hypercubicBoxGraph`; the AFM specialization `─ñ_{╬¢_n} = ╬ú_Γƒ¿x,yΓƒ⌐ ┼£_x┬╖┼£_y` (`J=1/2`, Tasaki unordered-bond convention) is the finite-volume model whose `LΓåæΓê₧` limit is the ┬º4.3 `InfiniteSpinSystem`. Hermitian for real `J`; ties to PR5's `hypercubicBoxCoupling` by `rfl`. Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxGroundEnergyS` / `boxGroundEnergyS_le_eigenvalues` / `boxBondCount` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS` | **finite-volume ground-state energy** `E_{GS,n}` (least eigenvalue of the Hermitian box AFM Hamiltonian, via `hermitianMinEigenvalue`), the **bond count** `\|B_n\|` (edges of `hypercubicBoxGraph`), and the **energy density** `E_{GS,n}/\|B_n\|` (Tasaki ┬º4.3 eq. (4.3.4)) ΓÇö the provable finite-volume groundwork for the thermodynamic-limit bridge (Issue #4564). Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxBondCount_pos` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto` | `boxBondCount_pos` (**proved**): for `0 < d`, `1 Γëñ n` the box has ΓëÑ1 bond (origin & `e_i` adjacent), so the energy-density denominator is positive on the tail (non-vacuity). `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto` (**documented AXIOM**, Tasaki ┬º4.3 eq. (4.3.4)): for `0 < d`, `0 < N` the finite-volume per-bond ground-state energy density converges as `nΓåÆΓê₧` to the infinite-volume energy density `╬╡_GS(d,N)` (existentially pinned to the genuine limit ΓÇö the thermodynamic-limit existence is the deep analytic content) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxGroundEnergyDensitySLimit` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto_limit` / `IsAFMThermodynamicLimit` / `afmThermodynamicLimit_energyDensity` / `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` | **thermodynamic-limit bridge** (Issue #4564 capstone): names the box energy-density limit `╬╡_GS(d,N)` (`boxGroundEnergyDensitySLimit`, proved to be the limit); the **documented predicate** `IsAFMThermodynamicLimit S N` (`S` is the `LΓåæΓê₧` limit of the concrete box AFM model ΓÇö not an identification of matrix algebras with the abstract C*-algebra); the **documented AXIOM** `afmThermodynamicLimit_energyDensity` (such `S`'s abstract energy density = the concrete box limit, Tasaki eq. (4.3.4)); the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` (symmetric infinite-volume ground state `╧ë_0` exists for the box AFM model's limit, via Theorem 4.20, eqs. (4.3.7)/(4.3.9)); and the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omegaN` (the symmetry-breaking ground states `╧ë_n` with N├⌐el magnetization `(ΓêÆ1)^x mΓêù n_╬▒`, given `HasStaggeredLRO`, eqs. (4.3.8)/(4.3.10) ΓÇö vacuous in 1D) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxThermodynamicLimit.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ┬╖ |ΓåæΓåæΓåæΓƒ⌐ = -J ┬╖ |ΓåæΓåæΓåæΓƒ⌐` ΓÇö confirming the linear scaling `E(|Γåæ..ΓåæΓƒ⌐) = -N┬╖J/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : Γäò`, `╬ú_{x,y Γêê Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ┬╖ J : Γäé)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|┬╖S┬▓` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N┬╖J/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : Γäò`, `J : Γä¥`, `k : Γäò`, `H_open ┬╖ ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬╖ |Γåæ..ΓåæΓƒ⌐) = -(N┬╖J/2 : Γäé) ┬╖ ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬╖ |Γåæ..ΓåæΓƒ⌐)` ΓÇö the unnormalised Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|╬ª_MΓƒ⌐` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|Γåô..ΓåôΓƒ⌐`, same eigenvalue `-(N┬╖J/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AΓÇôC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal ΓåÆ primitive (Seneta ┬º1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬º1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) Γëñ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬╖x Γëñ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬º1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬╖v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` Γƒ╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v ΓëÑ 0`, `v Γëá 0` Γƒ╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg Γƒ╣ Γêâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬º1.2, pp. 27ΓÇô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg Γƒ╣ Γêâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬º1.2, pp. 27ΓÇô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian Γƒ╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` Γƒ╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s ΓÇó v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ΓÇó u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `Γê¥ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u Γê¥ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬º1.2 (pp. 27ΓÇô28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallΓÇôLiebΓÇôMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ΓÇö the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼£Γü╗_tot)^M|all-upΓƒ⌐` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0ΓåÆ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronΓÇôFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─ñ, ┼£Γü╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ╧â : V ΓåÆ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ╧â = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). The two bundled sector-level MLM Theorem 2.2
ground-state results (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_groundState`,
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`) were
subsequently relocated to
`Quantum/SpinS/MarshallLiebMattisSectorBundled.lean` (#4569, re-proved via the
canonical `*Structural*` twins). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬ª` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2ΓêÆM)(card┬╖N/2ΓêÆMΓêÆ1)`, then `┼£Γü╗_tot ┬╖ magSectorEmbedding ╬ª` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─ñ, ┼£Γü╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronΓÇôFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼£Γü║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2ΓêÆK)(card┬╖N/2ΓêÆK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â = Γêæ_{xΓêêA}(N/2ΓêÆ╧â_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼£_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼£_A)┬▓`/`(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re Γëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re Γëñ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralPFJointCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬╖N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬╖N/2 ΓêÆ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A ΓêÆ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬╖N`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ Γëñ predicted ΓêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ΓêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness ΓÇö exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `╬╝`) has `╬╝ Γëñ E` for every sector eigenvector `╧å Γëá 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted ΓêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ΓêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_AΓêÆs_B|`, a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `╬│_A Γëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B Γëñ s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(╬│_totΓêÆ╬│_AΓêÆ╬│_B).re Γëñ predicted ΓêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ΓêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` has `╬│_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschΓÇôGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(┼£_tot)┬▓ ΓëÑ m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 Γëñ M.re` has `M.re┬╖(M.re+1) Γëñ ╬│.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼£Γü║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' ΓëÑ M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) ΓëÑ M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_AΓêÆs_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschΓÇôGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` at `(╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B)` is a `─ñ_toy`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot ΓêÆ ╬│_A ΓêÆ ╬│_B` (from `─ñ_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ΓêÆ(┼£_A)┬▓ΓêÆ(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(┼£_A)┬▓ Γëñ s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `╬│` of `(┼£_A)┬▓` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `╬│.re Γëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬╖N/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_AΓêÆk)`, repeatedly raising with `┼£_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `ΓèÑ`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at the same `╬│`. Sublattice analogue of `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (Issue #3658, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeightExistence.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(┼£_A)┬▓` commutes with `┼£_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_AΓêÆk) v` that is again a `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (Issue #3658, PR 10). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagWeightComponent.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagProjFn` / `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq` (with `sublatticeMagSumS`, `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_eq_sub`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice magnetization projection & decomposition**: the pointwise projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M v` (keeps `v ╧â` where `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â = M`) lands in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M`, is linear, and the projectors over the finite spectrum `s_A ΓêÆ k` (`k=0..|A|┬╖N`) sum to the identity (`sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â = s_A ΓêÆ sublatticeMagSumS A ╧â`, `sublatticeMagSumS A ╧â = Γêæ_{xΓêêA}╧â_x Γëñ |A|┬╖N`). Sublattice analogue of `magProjFn`/`sum_magProjFn_eq` (Issue #3658, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagProjection.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_im_zero`) | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir bound**: a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector (`┼£_A^+ w = 0`) at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` satisfies `╬│.re Γëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬╖N/2`. Its Casimir value is `M(M+1)` (#3663), `M` is an attained basis eigenvalue (#3667 ΓèÑ-lemma), so `|M| Γëñ s_A` (#3660). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (Issue #3658, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirHighestWeightBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_diag` / `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_off_diag` / `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum` | **Sublattice magnetization spectrum**: `┼£_A^(3)` is diagonal with entry `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â` on the diagonal and zero off it; hence a value `M` outside the diagonal spectrum gives `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M = ΓèÑ`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOp3_apply_diag/off_diag` and `magSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum`; lets the highest-weight argument conclude an attained sublattice magnetization is an actual basis eigenvalue (so `|M| Γëñ s_A`) (Issue #3658, PR 7). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagSpectrum.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` | **Sublattice ladder shifts the sublattice grading**: `┼£_A^+` maps `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M ΓåÆ A (M+1)` and `┼£_A^-` maps it to `A (MΓêÆ1)`, from the grading commutators `[┼£_A^(3), ┼£_A^┬▒] = ┬▒┼£_A^┬▒`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`; drives the termination of the highest-weight raising for `(┼£_A)┬▓` (Issue #3658, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagShift.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼£_A^(3) w = m_A┬╖w` and `┼£_A^+ w = 0`, then `(┼£_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_A+1)┬╖w`. From the sublattice Cartan identity `┼£_A^- ┼£_A^+ = (┼£_A^1)┬▓+(┼£_A^2)┬▓ΓêÆ┼£_A^(3)` (so `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^-┼£_A^+ + ┼£_A^(3) + (┼£_A^(3))┬▓`); on a highest-weight `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `┼£_A^-┼£_A^+` term vanishes. Sublattice analogue of the total highest-weight relation; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 4) for the sublattice Casimir spectral max bound. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice lowest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼£_A^(3) w = m_A┬╖w` and `┼£_A^- w = 0`, then `(┼£_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_AΓêÆ1)┬╖w`. From the other sublattice Cartan identity `┼£_A^+ ┼£_A^- = (┼£_A^1)┬▓+(┼£_A^2)┬▓+┼£_A^(3)` (so `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^+┼£_A^- ΓêÆ ┼£_A^(3) + (┼£_A^(3))┬▓`); on a lowest-weight vector the `┼£_A^+┼£_A^-` term vanishes. At `m_A = ΓêÆs_A` this gives `(┼£_A)┬▓ = s_A(s_A+1)` (max), so the all-down sublattice state realizes the maximal `(┼£_A)┬▓` ΓÇö a building block of the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLowestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_antiAligned_eq_max` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_complement_antiAligned_eq_max` (with `antiAlignedConfigS`) | **Anti-aligned state realizes the maximal sublattice Casimirs**: the basis config `antiAlignedConfigS A N` (all `A`-sites up `╧â=0`, all `┬¼A`-sites down `╧â=N`) satisfies `(┼£_A)┬▓ |╧âΓéÇΓƒ⌐ = s_A(s_A+1) |╧âΓéÇΓƒ⌐` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓ |╧âΓéÇΓƒ⌐ = s_B(s_B+1) |╧âΓéÇΓƒ⌐` ΓÇö the `A`-block is the sublattice highest weight (#3663), the `┬¼A`-block the sublattice lowest weight (#3681). Total magnetization `s_A ΓêÆ s_B` (the extremal sector); projecting onto `(┼£_tot)┬▓=predicted` yields the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23AntialignedJointEigenvector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_highestWeight_totalCasimir_eq_predicted` | **Extremal-sector highest weight has the predicted total Casimir**: a vector in the extremal magnetization sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬╖N` annihilated by `┼£Γü║_tot` is a `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N` (the total highest-weight relation gives `m(m+1)` with `m = |s_AΓêÆs_B|`, via #3678). With the maximal sublattice Casimirs, such a highest-weight vector is the minimal-total-spin joint eigenstate; its existence (ClebschΓÇôGordan) is the last obligation (Issue #3674, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalHighestWeight.lean`) |
| `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace_totalSpinSOpPlus_invariant` / `_totalSpinSOpMinus_invariant` | **Ladder invariance of the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace**: `W = jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` (`(┼£_A)┬▓=s_A(s_A+1)` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓=s_B(s_B+1)`) is invariant under `┼£Γü║_tot` and `┼£Γü╗_tot` (both sublattice Casimirs commute with them). `W` is the carrier on which `(┼£_tot)┬▓` decomposes; the rank-nullity of `┼£Γü║_tot` across its magnetization grading yields the minimal-total-spin highest-weight state (Issue #3674, PR 10). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/JointCasimirEigenspaceLadderInvariant.lean`) |
| `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace_totalSpinSOp3_invariant` | **`┼£┬│_tot`-invariance of the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace**: `W = jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` is invariant under `┼£┬│_tot` (both sublattice Casimirs commute with it), so it is graded by total magnetization `W = Γèò_M (W Γèô magSubspaceS V N M)`. With the ladder invariance (#3684), `W` is an `su(2)`-subrepresentation ΓÇö the setting for the rank-nullity argument producing the minimal-total-spin highest-weight state (Issue #3674, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/JointCasimirEigenspaceMagInvariant.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspace_ne_bot` (with `allAlignedStateS_zero_mem_sublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspace`) | **The maximal sublattice-Casimir eigenspace is non-trivial**: the `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenspace at `s_A(s_A+1)` (the A-symmetric subspace) contains the non-zero all-up state `|╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquaredEigenspace_max_ne_bot`; the base of the sublattice-symmetric-subspace dimension theory producing the minimal-total-spin highest-weight state (Issue #3687, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspaceNeBot.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspace_complement_ne_bot` | **The complement maximal sublattice-Casimir eigenspace is non-trivial**: the `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`-eigenspace at `s_B(s_B+1)` contains the non-zero all-up state. Complement companion of `sublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspace_ne_bot`, via the negated sublattice (Issue #3687, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspaceComplementNeBot.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspace_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_invariant` / `_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_invariant` | **Ladder invariance of the maximal sublattice-Casimir eigenspace**: the `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenspace (any eigenvalue `M`) is invariant under the sublattice ladders `┼£_A^+`, `┼£_A^-` (the sublattice Casimir commutes with them). With non-triviality (#3688), the A-symmetric subspace contains the whole `┼£_A^-`-ladder orbit of the all-up state ΓÇö its spanning set with one state per `A`-magnetization (Issue #3687, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMaxCasimirEigenspaceLadderInvariant.lean`) |
| `sublatticeLadderIterateDownS` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeLadderIterateDownS` | **Sublattice ladder iterates of the all-up state**: `(┼£_A^-)^k ┬╖ |╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐` lies in the maximal `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenspace (`(┼£_A)┬▓` commutes with every power of `┼£_A^-`, and `|╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐` realizes the maximal Casimir). These iterates (one per `A`-magnetization) are the spanning family of the A-symmetric subspace; sublattice analogue of `ladderIterateUp` (┬º2.4) (Issue #3687, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLadderIterate.lean`) |
| `sublatticeLadderIterateDownS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_allUp`) | **Sublattice magnetization of the ladder iterates**: `(┼£_A^-)^k ┬╖ |╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐ Γêê sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_A ΓêÆ k)` (the all-up state has `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenvalue `s_A`, and each `┼£_A^-` lowers the sublattice magnetization by one). Distinct `k` give distinct sublattice magnetizations, so the iterates are linearly independent once non-vanishing is established ΓÇö the dimension-counting input for the A-symmetric subspace (Issue #3687, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLadderIterateMag.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_sublatticeLadderIterateDownS_succ` | **Sublattice raising-of-lowering identity**: `┼£_A^+ (┼£_A^-)^{k+1} \|╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐ = (k+1)(\|A\|┬╖N ΓêÆ k) (┼£_A^-)^k \|╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐`. The `su(2)` ladder identity for the `A`-sublattice on the all-up state, from the sublattice Cartan `┼£_A^+ ┼£_A^- = (┼£_A)┬▓ ΓêÆ (┼£_A^(3))┬▓ + ┼£_A^(3)`, the maximal Casimir (#3691) and the iterate's magnetization `s_A ΓêÆ k` (#3692). Since `(k+1)(\|A\|┬╖N ΓêÆ k) Γëá 0` for `k < \|A\|┬╖N`, it drives the inductive non-vanishing of the iterates; sublattice analogue of ┬º2.4's total identity (Issue #3687, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLadderIdentity.lean`) |
| `sublatticeLadderIterateDownS_ne_zero` | **Non-vanishing of the sublattice ladder iterates**: for `k Γëñ |A|┬╖N`, `(┼£_A^-)^k ┬╖ |╧â_ΓèñΓƒ⌐ Γëá 0`. Inductive (sublattice analogue of ┬º2.4's total non-vanishing): if `v_{k+1}=0` then `┼£_A^+ v_{k+1} = (k+1)(|A|┬╖NΓêÆk) v_k = 0` (the ladder identity #3693), and the scalar is non-zero for `k < |A|┬╖N`, forcing `v_k = 0` against the IH. With the distinct magnetizations (#3692), the `|A|┬╖N+1` iterates are linearly independent (Issue #3687, PR 7). Hence `finrank` of the A-symmetric subspace is ΓëÑ `|A|┬╖N+1`; sublattice analogue of `ladderIterateUp_linearIndependent` (┬º2.4) (Issue #3687, PR 8). Since `┼£Γü╗_tot` commutes with all three Casimir operators (`totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`) the eigenvalues are preserved, and lowering is non-vanishing because the predicted value differs from the sector's lowering-kernel value. Hence the magnetization component of an `(┼£_A)┬▓`- (resp. `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`-) eigenvector is again a Casimir eigenvector at the same eigenvalue ΓÇö the Casimir-inheritance needed for the bottom-component extraction of the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). | `exists_sublattice_A_lowestWeight_component` | **Bottom sublattice-`A` magnetization component is `┼£_A^-`-killed** (option (a), Route 5 brick 2b): a total lowest-weight `w` (`┼£Γü╗_tot w = 0`, total magnetization `m`, simultaneous sublattice-Casimir eigenvector at `╬▒, ╬▓`) yields a non-zero `┼£_A^-`-killed simultaneous weight vector `v` with `┼£_A^(3) v = p v`, `┼£_┬¼A^(3) v = (mΓêÆp) v` and the inherited Casimir eigenvalues `╬▒, ╬▓`. Obtained as the minimal-`A`-magnetization nonzero component (max over the magnetization-level projections): projecting `┼£Γü╗_tot w = ┼£_A^- w + ┼£_┬¼A^- w = 0` onto the level just below `p` leaves only `┼£_A^- v` (the `┼£_┬¼A^-` part preserves `A`-magnetization and sees only the vanishing lower component). This is the key construction of the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23SublatticeBottomComponent.lean`) |
| `totalLowestWeight_re_le_complement_sub` | **One-sided total-magnetization bound** (option (a), Route 5 brick 3a): a total lowest weight `w` in the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace with spins `a, b` (`(┼£_A)┬▓ = a(a+1)`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓ = b(b+1)`) has `m.re Γëñ b ΓêÆ a`. The bottom-`A`-component (#brick 2b) is `┼£_A^-`-killed at `A`-weight `p`, so `p Γëñ 0` (brick 1) and `p(pΓêÆ1) = a(a+1)` (lowest-weight Casimir) force `p Γëñ ΓêÆa`; the magnitude bound on `┬¼A` (brick 0) gives `m ΓêÆ p Γëñ b`, whence `m.re Γëñ b + p.re Γëñ b ΓêÆ a`. Combined with its `A Γåö ┬¼A` mirror this yields `|m| ΓëÑ |a ΓêÆ b|` ΓÇö the ClebschΓÇôGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3542). | `exists_total_lowestWeight_joint` | **Total lowest-weight existence with joint Casimir eigenvalues** (option (a), Route 5 brick 3b-engine): a non-zero simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` reaches, by repeatedly lowering with `┼£Γü╗_tot`, a non-zero `┼£Γü╗_tot`-killed vector at the *same* three eigenvalues (the sublattice Casimirs are preserved since `┼£Γü╗_tot` commutes with them, #3714). Induction on the magnetization level down to the bottom (where `┼£Γü╗_tot` annihilates, `magEigenvalueS_ne_neg_mMax_sub_one`). This is the reduction to the lowest weight for the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). Reducing to a total lowest weight `w'` (#3722) at the same three eigenvalues, the one-sided magnetization bound (#3721) applied to both `A` and `┬¼A` gives `M.re Γëñ bΓêÆa` and `M.re Γëñ aΓêÆb`, so `|M.re| ΓëÑ |aΓêÆb|`; the total lowest-weight Casimir relation `(┼£_tot)┬▓ w' = M(MΓêÆ1) w'` then gives `╬│.re = M.re┬▓ ΓêÆ M.re ΓëÑ |aΓêÆb|(|aΓêÆb|+1)`. Since `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^(1)┬▓ + ┼£_A^(2)┬▓ + ┼£_A^(3)┬▓` is a sum of squares of Hermitian operators, `Γƒ¿v, (┼£_A)┬▓ vΓƒ⌐ = ╬ú_╬▒ ΓÇû┼£_A^(╬▒) vΓÇû┬▓ ΓëÑ 0`. The first lemma gives the direct eigenspace identification; the second specializes PR #3896 to the SU(2) point, giving `┼£┬│_tot|╬ªΓƒ⌐=0` for any non-zero GS of the isotropic Heisenberg Hamiltonian under the GS uniqueness hypothesis. Prepares the SU(2) base case for the deformation argument: once uniqueness is established at the SU(2) point (for symmetric `|A|=|┬¼A|` via Theorem 2.2 + SU(2) singlet structure, separate forthcoming work), the `┼£┬│_tot|╬ªΓƒ⌐=0` conclusion follows immediately. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43-44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem24SU2BaseCase.lean`) |

Proof: evaluate `H ┬╖ ┼£┬│_tot = ┼£┬│_tot ┬╖ H` at `(╧â, ╧ä)`; since `┼£┬│_tot` is diagonal with entries `magEigenvalueS`, get `H ╧â ╧ä ┬╖ M_╧ä = M_╧â ┬╖ H ╧â ╧ä`. When `magSumS ╧â Γëá magSumS ╧ä`, Nat-cast injectivity gives `magEigenvalueS ╧â Γëá magEigenvalueS ╧ä`, so `H ╧â ╧ä = 0`. This makes the same-site Marshall sign `(ΓêÆ1)^{╧â'_x + ╧â_x} = +1` on the support of the single-ion term, so the dressed single-ion off-diagonal entries keep the sign `ΓêÆD/4 Γëñ 0`. Per site the `(┼£┬▓)┬▓` entry vanishes unless the shift is `┬▒2` (even), where the same-site Marshall sign (`marshallSignS_mul_of_agree_off_site`) is `+1`, leaving the non-positive `(┼£┬▓)┬▓` off-diagonal entry (`SingleIonOffDiag`); multiplying by `D ΓëÑ 0` and summing over the (single) differing site preserves the sign. Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank Γäé (eig M.submatrix ╬╜) = finrank Γäé (eig M ╬╜ Γèô ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ╬╜-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig Γèô ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. For finite-dim `T, P` with `TΓêÿP = PΓêÿT` and `PΓêÿP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `╬╝` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `┬▒1`. The `Γëñ` was tight because the two intersected `┬▒1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Central tool for unconditional `Γëñ 2` at any ╬╝ where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(╬╜_0, ╬╜_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `─ñ'` GS degeneracy `Γëñ 2`. Proof via spectrum-eigenvalues bridge (`HasEigenvalue.mem_spectrum` ΓåÆ `Matrix.spectrum_toLin'` ΓåÆ `spectrum.algebraMap_mem_iff` ΓåÆ `IsHermitian.spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`). Given per-block bounds `finrank Γëñ 1` at the per-block minimum eigenvalues, the full-eigenspace at `min(min_0, min_1)` has `finrank Γëñ 2`. Case analysis: `min_0 = min_1` both contribute `Γëñ 1`; `min_0 Γëá min_1` the higher block contributes `0` by (i.4) #3849, the lower `Γëñ 1`. Bridge from per-block PF simplicity to the unconditional global ground-state degeneracy `Γëñ 2` (modulo identification of PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`). | `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_truly_unconditional` | **≡ƒÄ» (#3887.8) Spin-1/2 anisotropic `─ñ` eigenspace `Γëñ 2` (TRULY UNCONDITIONAL)** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3887, PR #3888): combines (#3887.7) at parity 0/1 with (j.12) #3870 spin-1/2 capstone. **Non-vacuous closure of (j.13.i)** ΓÇö the spin-1/2 case now has a usable bound without the `h_intermediate` caveat. **MILESTONE**: this is the non-vacuous spin-1/2 closure of obligation (1) of Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4. | `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS_anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general` | **General spin-S axis-swap discharge for Theorem 2.4 obligation (1)** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): the concrete `╧Ç/2` rotation about spin-axis 1, `axisSwapUnitarySSpinS N`, now instantiates the earlier `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` interface for every spin parameter `N`. The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `Γëñ 2` bounds, the min-block `Γëñ 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43ΓÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AxisSwapUnitarySSpinS.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank Γëñ 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank Γëñ 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank Γëñ 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank Γëñ 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `┼£┬│` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43ΓÇô44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetConditionalBridge.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSBalancedSectorPFTarget.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergParametricPath_in_case_ii_region` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S case-(ii) path and conditional target bridge** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that the deformation path from `(1,0)` to a target satisfying `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` remains in the case-(ii) region on `[0,1]`, then repackages the balanced-sector PF target bridge under case-(ii) hypotheses. These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIConditionalBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from strict sector gap** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): composes the arbitrary-parameter strict-gap target bridge with the case-(ii) theorem surface. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input from the first case-(ii) wrappers is replaced by the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` over every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector under `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`; the remaining full ground eigenspace `finrank <= 2` input stays explicit in this intermediate wrapper. This is a case-(ii) bridge step, not the final case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_groundState_mem_balanced_sector_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` | **General spin-S target bridge from strict sector gap without the full `finrank <= 2` input** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that if the balanced sector attains the full ground energy and every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector has strictly larger minimum, then every full ground vector lies in the balanced magnetization sector. This uses the sector-projection eigenvector bridge and `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min`, not the case-(i) parity-block `finrank <= 2` contradiction. Composing this containment with balanced-sector Perron--Frobenius simplicity gives full target uniqueness and zero total magnetization from strict sector gap alone; the case-(ii)-named wrappers keep only the theorem-facing `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` hypotheses plus the strict-gap input. This removes the explicit case-(ii) full `finrank <= 2` input; deriving the case-(ii) strict gap remains future work. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSTargetFromStrictGapNoFullFinrank.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_strict_gap_all_M_of_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_crossing_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) strict-gap and target bridge from a crossing contradiction callback** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): compresses the remaining case-(ii) strict-gap derivation to one theorem-shaped callback. If every non-balanced target crossing `E_M <= E_balanced` contradicts the case-(ii) hypotheses `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`, then order duality gives the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` for every non-balanced non-empty sector. The target wrappers feed that strict gap to the no-full-finrank bridge above, so the public endpoint surface no longer exposes either balanced/full equality, full `finrank <= 2`, or strict gap directly; the remaining mathematical task is proving the crossing contradiction callback. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapFromCrossing.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_crossing_contradiction_of_path_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_path_crossing_contradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_path_crossing_contradiction` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from a path crossing contradiction callback** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): evaluates the case-(ii) deformation path at `t = 1`. If every crossing along the path inside the region `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` contradicts the path hypotheses, then a target crossing `E_M(lambda,D) <= E_balanced(lambda,D)` is impossible. The target wrappers feed this contradiction to the crossing-callback bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the path crossing contradiction callback itself. Therefore, if every non-balanced non-empty sector has contradictory crossing-set non-emptiness, every path crossing is impossible, and the target wrappers feed that path contradiction to the path-callback bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the crossing-set non-emptiness contradiction callback, matching the first-crossing `hne` surface used by the existing scaffold. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIPathCrossingFromSet.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_crossing_set_contradiction_of_first_crossing` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_first_crossing` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_first_crossing` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from a first-crossing contradiction** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): non-emptiness of `perMCrossingSet M Γê⌐ Icc 0 1` gives the achieved first crossing `sInf (perMCrossingSet M Γê⌐ Icc 0 1)` by `sInf_perMCrossingSet_inter_Icc_mem`. Thus a contradiction at that first-crossing point rules out crossing-set non-emptiness, and the target wrappers feed the result to the crossing-set bridge above. The remaining mathematical task is now the first-crossing contradiction callback itself. | `caseIIArgminFirstCrossingContradiction` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_crossing_contradiction_of_argmin_first_crossing` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_argmin_first_crossing` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_argmin_first_crossing` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from the argmin first crossing** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): a target crossing at `t = 1` gives a non-empty crossing set; `exists_M_chosen_argmin_per_M_first_crossing` then selects a non-balanced sector whose first crossing minimises `sInf (perMCrossingSet M Γê⌐ Icc 0 1)` among all non-empty crossing sectors. The named callback `caseIIArgminFirstCrossingContradiction` only has to contradict this selected argmin sector, with the argmin property available. The target wrappers feed the resulting target crossing contradiction to the crossing-callback bridge. The remaining mathematical task is now the argmin first-crossing contradiction callback itself. The remaining explicit input is a full ground eigenspace `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first-crossing parameter, plus the standard SU(2)-point strict-gap/ground-state inputs and the centered-magnetization nonzero bookkeeping for non-balanced sectors. The target wrappers feed this callback to the argmin-first-crossing bridge above. | `caseII_first_crossing_finrank_bound_of_path_global_finrank_bound` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_path_global_finrank_bound` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_path_global_finrank_bound` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from a path-global `finrank <= 2` bound** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): repackages the selected first-crossing finrank input from the previous row.
